title: AGM/DGM-Operations (JD-0026)
description: **Overview:** AGM/ DGM – Operations is responsible for overseeing and managing the day\-to\-day administrative and operational functions of the hospital to ensure high\-quality patient care, operational efficiency, and compliance with healthcare regulations. The role demands strong leadership, problem\-solving, and communication skills, as well as the ability to drive operational excellence in a fast\-paced, patient\-centric environment. **Key Responsibilities \& Tasks** * Oversee day\-to\-day operations of departments including OPD, IPD, diagnostics, pharmacy, support services, and facilities. * Ensure effective optimize resource utilization, patient flow, and turnaround times. * Liaise with department to monitor clinical \& non\-clinical operations and resolve escalations. * collaborate with leadership to develop and execute operational strategies aligned with hospital goal. * Recruitment, evaluation, training, performance appraisal and retention of qualified staff. * Implement new initiatives and monitor KPIs to improve performance and efficiency. * Ensure regulatory compliance, accreditation readiness (NABH/NABL/JCI), Clinical indicators, patient care, facility management, and quality improvement. * Monitor patient feedback and grievance redressal mechanisms to enhance service quality. * Assist in Budget preparation and financial planning for operations. * Oversee quality assurance programs \& patient safety initiatives * Oversee facility management including housekeeping, security, maintenance, and biomedical equipment. * Manage vendor relationships and service contracts to ensure cost\-effective procurement and service quality. * Enhance patient satisfaction through prompt services, streamlined admission/discharge processes, and compassionate care. * Coordinate with the front office, billing, and medical records to ensure seamless patient journeys. * Maintain regular reporting of key operational metrics, utilization, patient volumes, and cost drivers. * Present monthly and quarterly dashboards to senior leadership for performance review **Qualification \& Experience** * Any graduate/ Postgraduate with 15\+ years of progressive hospital operations experience in 300 \+ bedded hospital settings. * Regular Master’s degree in Hospital Administration (MHA) or MBA (Hospital Management), or equivalent is preferred. * Training in leadership, quality assurance, or healthcare management is desirable. * Experience with hospital quality standards, Vendor audits, and policy implementation. * Familiarity with hospital accreditation (NABH/NBL/JCI) procedures and compliance requirements. **Remuneration:** As per industry standards **Why Join Us:** At Sant Nirankari Health City, we offer a collaborative and supportive work environment where your contributions are valued and recognized. Join us in our mission to deliver exceptional healthcare services while upholding the highest standards of quality and patient care. Apply Now ← Back to Careers Page

title: AI Operations Champion
description: Reporting to the Head of Operations Transformation, The AI Operations Champion plays a critical role in embedding AI‑enabled automation into day‑to‑day operational delivery across Waystone. The role acts as the bridge between Operations teams and technology, ensuring that manual, repetitive processes are identified, prioritised, and systematically removed through the application of AI and automation. The AI Operations Champion is responsible for owning automation outcomes within their operational area, supporting use‑case discovery, contributing to solution design, and ensuring that automated processes are adopted, governed, and sustained in BAU. The role is central to delivering operating leverage, improving scalability, and future‑proofing both Waystone’s operating model and its people. **Essential Duties And Responsibilities** Decision Making \& Judgment * Act as the primary operations representative for AI‑led automation initiatives within the assigned business area. * Identify, assess, and prioritise manual, repetitive, and error‑prone operational activities suitable for automation. * Contribute to the design of AI and automation solutions, ensuring they are operationally sound, scalable, and aligned with agreed standards and guardrails. * Exercise judgment in determining when automation is appropriate versus when process redesign or data remediation is required. * Support benefit identification and value tracking to ensure automation initiatives deliver measurable, repeatable impact. Curiosity \& Learning * Maintain a strong understanding of Waystone’s AI and automation tooling, patterns, and capabilities. * Actively seek opportunities to improve existing processes through the application of AI, automation, and reusable components. * Stay informed on emerging AI use cases relevant to fund administration, operations, and client servicing. * Participate in training, accreditation, and knowledge‑sharing initiatives related to AI‑enabled operations. Enthusiastic Teamwork * Work collaboratively with Operations, Technology, Risk, Compliance, and the AI \& Automation CoE to deliver automation outcomes. * Support colleagues in understanding how AI‑enabled automation changes roles, workflows, and responsibilities. * Contribute to cross‑functional initiatives aimed at improving operational efficiency and scalability. * Assist with ad hoc projects and pilots where capacity permits. Courageous Leadership * Champion a shift from manual process execution to process ownership and design, encouraging teams to embrace new ways of working. * Act as a visible advocate for AI‑enabled automation within Operations, challenging legacy practices constructively. * Share lessons learned, success stories, and improvement opportunities with peers and leadership. * Support the change management required to embed automation into BAU operations. Self‑Responsibility * Develop and maintain a detailed understanding of end‑to‑end operational processes within the assigned area. * Document automation opportunities, process changes, and ownership models clearly and accurately. * Ensure automated processes have clear BAU ownership, controls, and escalation paths. * Take accountability for the ongoing performance and effectiveness of automated processes within Operations. Selfless Customer Service * Ensure AI‑enabled automation enhances service quality, accuracy, and timeliness for internal and external stakeholders. * Support consistent standards for operational delivery as automation is introduced and scaled. * Work with relevant teams to ensure automation aligns with regulatory, risk, and control requirements. **Requirements** Experience * Strong operational experience within financial services or a similar regulated environment. * Demonstrated experience improving or redesigning operational processes. * Exposure to automation, workflow tools, AI use cases, or process digitisation is highly desirable. * Ability to work effectively across functions and influence without direct authority. * Strong communication skills, with the ability to translate operational problems into structured improvement opportunities. * Analytical mindset with attention to detail and a focus on outcomes. * Proficient in standard productivity tools; experience working alongside automation or low‑code platforms is an advantage. Education * A third‑level qualification in Business, Finance, Technology, or a related discipline is desirable.

title: AI for QA Teaching Expert (B2B) Part-time
description: **Nebius Academy** is an educational platform that helps experienced software developers stay at the forefront of technological innovation. We create specialized courses that teach professionals how to effectively use AI tools in real\-world development workflows. Our programs are designed to help engineers critically and productively apply artificial intelligence in their professional work, improving code quality, development speed, and overall work efficiency. **Our mission:** Provide engineers with access to cutting\-edge knowledge in AI\-assisted development and help them become leaders in the new era of programming. We're building a hands\-on workshop series for experienced QA engineers on the practical use of AI\-assisted testing tools in real\-world QA workflows. As the Expert Instructor, you will lead interactive live sessions that go beyond passive learning, helping QA engineers integrate AI tools into their day\-to\-day practice. Your audience will be B2B clients: skilled QA professionals working in fintech environments — payments, banking, card systems, and adjacent domains — focused on improving coverage, efficiency, and testing quality through AI\-assisted workflows. We are seeking **experienced developers and technical experts** (5\+ years) with hands\-on AI tools experience, a proven track record in tech leadership or mentoring, and the ability to explain complex concepts clearly. Ideal candidates are fluent in English, detail\-oriented, able to work independently, and can commit 10\-15 hours per week to creating and/or presenting educational content for professional developers. **Please submit your resume in English.** What you will do: * Conduct live, interactive training sessions and workshops; * Create clear, concise content for lessons, manuals, and assessments — including texts, draft slides, and screencasts; * Participate as a speaker in learning videos and design the course's final project; * Review and incorporate learner feedback to continuously improve session and content design; * Work with the curriculum team to ensure strong alignment between async and live content; * Communicate with students during Q\&A sessions. ⌛ This role requires 10\-15 hours of availability per week. Requirements: * 5\+ years of professional experience in QA engineering; * Hands\-on experience with AI\-assisted QA tools and proven cases of their successful implementation; * Comfort with test strategy, automation approaches, and modern QA tooling in high\-compliance, high\-reliability environments; * Background in engineer advocacy, tech leadership, conference participation, or mentoring. * Ability to translate complex QA and fintech concepts into actionable, engaging learning experiences; * Russian language (some of our students speak Russian); * Fluent spoken and written English; * Confident, collaborative, and audience\-oriented facilitation style; * Strong attention to detail and ability to work independently; * Time commitment: able to devote \~10 hours per week. **Nice to have:** * Background in fintech QA (payments, banking, card systems, or adjacent domains); * Experience teaching or coaching senior QA engineers or engineering teams in fintech companies; * Familiarity with remote collaboration tools (Zoom, Miro, Slack, etc.); * Personal experience as an online learner; * Interest in instructional design and education. What we can offer you: * The opportunity to create impactful content while maintaining your primary job: Share your expertise without leaving your current role * Competitive hourly rate of $40\-$80 USD for flexible part\-time collaboration with significant impact and an amazing team! * Remote cooperation with a schedule convenient for both you and the team: We don't focus on micromanagement * Cross\-cultural experience: Become part of an international team and connect with professionals from diverse backgrounds * Meaningful impact: Share your knowledge and help experienced engineers advance their skills through high\-quality educational content * Participation in innovative projects: Contribute to shaping the future of programming education and AI adoption * Professional growth: Receive feedback and develop your skills as a technical content creator and thought leader

title: ASPIRE GSC – Service Desk Level 1/Level 2 Analyst
description: **Company Description** Version 1 has celebrated 30 years in business and continues to be trusted by global brands to deliver technology and transformation solutions that drive customer success. Our deep expertise enables our customers to navigate the rapidly evolving technology landscape. We foster strong partnerships with global technology leaders including Microsoft, AWS, Oracle, Red Hat, OutSystems, Snowflake, ensuring that our customers are provided with the highest quality solutions and services. We’re an award\-winning employer reflecting how our employees are at the very heart of what we do: * UK \& Ireland's premier AWS, Microsoft \& Oracle partner * 3300\+ strong, €350/£300m revenue business * 10\+ years as a Great Place to Work in Ireland \& UK * Best Workplace for Women in the UK \& Ireland by GPTW * Best Workplace for Wellbeing in the UK by GPTW We’re a core values driven company, we hire people who share our values, and we reward those who display and foster them, it’s deeply embedded within our DNA. Invest in us and we’ll invest in you!. **Job Description** **Role Summary** Our ASPIRE Global Service Centre is the central hub of our Service Management operations. Beyond a traditional Service Desk, it stands as the central authority and shared service delivery hub, orchestrating all operational workflows, processes, procedures, and tooling. It’s a core delivery component of the Version 1 ASPIRE Managed Services offering that places AI, continuous improvement and business innovation at the heart of everything Version 1 does. With a focus on supporting self\-service and automation, we utilise the best digital capabilities of the ServiceNow ITSM tooling product to provide the very best experience to our customers. We are seeking an experienced and results\-driven Level 1/Level 2 IT Service Desk Analyst. As an IT Service Desk Analyst, you are the first point of contact for technical support within the organization. Your role is to provide timely and professional assistance to resolve IT\-related issues, helping your colleagues and our customers stay productive and meet their objectives. You take pride in delivering excellent customer service, applying your technical expertise, and ensuring a reliable IT infrastructure. Whether handling first\-level queries or escalating more complex challenges or performing maintenance activities, you are committed to maintaining a smooth and efficient IT environment. The successful candidate will play a critical role in managing and resolving complex technical issues within our Azure, AWS, Microsoft Office, and Windows service environments. This role demands a strong understanding of cloud technologies, exceptional problem\-solving skills, and the ability to provide exceptional support to our clients. **Key Responsibilities:** * Serve as the first point of contact for technical support via phone, email, or chat. * Log and manage incidents, service requests, and queries in the ticketing system. * Perform initial triage, diagnosis and troubleshooting for IT issues, including hardware, software, and network problems. * Escalate unresolved issues to higher support levels or specialized teams as needed. * Provide support for user account management, such as password resets and access requests. * Ensure timely resolution of incidents in line with Service Level Agreements (SLAs). * Maintain accurate documentation of incidents, resolutions, and troubleshooting steps. * Contribute to the development and maintenance of the IT knowledge base. * Deliver excellent customer service and build strong relationships with end\-users. * Monitor system performance and identify potential issues proactively. * Collaborate with other IT teams to address complex or recurring problems. * Participate in IT projects, such as system upgrades or deployments, when required. * Conduct routine health checks of systems, applications, and networks to ensure optimal performance. * Perform scheduled maintenance, such as software updates, patch installations, and backups. * Monitor and manage system alerts to address potential issues before they escalate. * Review and refine service desk documentation and processes regularly for efficiency and accuracy. * Test business continuity measures, including disaster recovery plans, to ensure readiness. * Collaborate with other IT team members to ensure timely resolution of issues and continuous improvement of service delivery. * Participate in ongoing training and development to stay current with IT trends and technologies. * Flexibility to work in a rotating shift pattern, including nights, weekends, and holidays. **Qualifications** **Skills, Education \& Qualifications:** * Minimum of 2\-5 years of experience in IT support * Experience in a service desk support role. * Strong understanding of IT infrastructure, systems, and troubleshooting methodologies. * Experience with service desk management tools (e.g., ServiceNow). * Knowledge of ITIL frameworks and best practices; ITIL v4 certification preferred. * Strong communication, interpersonal, and problem\-solving abilities. * Customer\-focused mindset with a commitment to delivering high\-quality support. * Strategic thinking and decision\-making. * Ability to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines. * Adaptability to evolving technologies and processes. * Strong analytical and reporting skills. * Proficiency in Microsoft Office 365 Support. * Knowledge of AWS and Azure fundamentals. * Proficiency in Active Directory and Group Policy Support * Technical proficiency in Windows Server and Linux (preferable) operating systems. **Additional Information** **Why Version 1?** At Version 1, we believe in providing our employees with a comprehensive benefits package that prioritises their wellbeing, professional growth, and financial stability. * Share in our success with our Quarterly Performance\-Related Profit Share Scheme, where employees collectively benefit from a share of our company's profits * Strong Career Progression \& mentorship coaching through our Strength in Balance \& Leadership schemes with a dedicated quarterly Pathways Career Development programme * Flexible/remote working, Version 1 is tremendously understanding of life events and people’s individual circumstances and offer flexibility to help achieve a healthy work life balance * Financial Wellbeing initiatives including; Pension, Private Healthcare Cover, Life Assurance, Financial advice and an Employee Discount scheme * Employee Wellbeing schemes including Gym Discounts, Bike to Work, Fitness classes, Mindfulness Workshops, Employee Assistance Programme and much more. Generous holiday allowance, enhanced maternity/paternity leave, marriage/civil partnership leave and special leave policies * Educational assistance, incentivised certifications, and accreditations, including AWS, Microsoft, Oracle, and Red Hat * Reward schemes including Version 1’s Annual Excellence Awards \& ‘Call\-Out’ platform. * Environment, Social and Community First initiatives allow you to get involved in local fundraising and development opportunities as part of fostering our diversity, inclusion and belonging schemes. And many more exciting benefits… drop us a note to find out more. Version 1 is an equal opportunities employer. We are committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels valued and able to thrive. We welcome applications from people of all backgrounds, identities and lived experiences, and we value the different perspectives people bring including those shaped by disability and neurodiversity. We want every candidate to have a positive and accessible recruitment experience. If you need reasonable adjustments at any stage of the process, please contact your recruiter at Version 1\. We will consider all requests carefully, respectfully and confidentially. Video links: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v\=F\_d3ELTH5zo

title: AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin/Remote (2 days onsite pw)
description: **AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin/Remote (2 days onsite pw)** My client, based in Dublin City, is a leader in providing technology to Life Science regulatory processes and is continuing to expand its Platform team. They have partnered with IT Search to engage a mid\-level AWS DevOps Engineer to work alongside Engineering Heads and work on complex issues relating to automation, CI/CD, business logic, security and design matters. ***The role:*** * Develop strong internal relationships with Developers and Quality teams to deliver architecture on an ongoing basis, working closely with Architects to execute pipelines * Focus on areas such as security at scale, taking ownership of high quality output across deployment and create/manage/amend CI/CD pipelines to a deep level * Automation through (Python or similar) * There will be some involvement in incident response when required * Evidence based attitude to work, ability to back up ideas and recognise errors and change course accordingly * Proven experience of AWS Services, Pipelining, Kubernetes, Terraform and Python ***Role Requirements:*** * Approximately 5 years of commercial experience in a Platform/DevOps Environment * Ideally have worked in depth with Kubernetes Clusters (maintaining as well as building rather than simply deploying), AWS Services (as a preference), IaC (Terraform ideally), Python/Go on a daily basis for ongoing scripting and automation This is a high potential position with a well\-established firm to join a highly experienced and talented team, with a strong benefits package and work life balance. To learn more, please share your latest CV to arrange a confidential conversation, however, please note we can only accept applications from candidates eligible to work on a permanent basis in Ireland.

title: Accommodation Assistant
description: Start your career at Dalata Hotel Group! We are looking for team members to join us on an exciting journey. You will receive training through our Dalata Academy to help you learn important skills. You will also meet experienced team members who can help you grow. At Dalata, we believe in promoting from within. If you work hard, you can have new opportunities in the future. Join us and be part of a friendly team! **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off F\&B in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata\-owned hotels * 30% off Friends \& Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, fun team\-building activities and regular charity events **Your Job** * Ensure that all rooms and common areas are cleaned to the Dalata standard. * Point out anything that needs fixing. * Be ready to help in other departments. **What You’ll Need** * Having experience in cleaning and housekeeping is good, but it is not needed. * Friendly with customers * Pay attention to details. **About Us** Dalata Hotel Group – We bring *the heart of hospitality* to every hotel we own and operate. We see our growth and expansion plans as a way of spreading our unique way of caring for our guests further around the world, bringing better experiences to more people. Currently, the Dalata difference can be felt at each of our hotels in Ireland, the UK, Germany \& the Netherlands, and we’ll keep expanding this further, focusing on key UK cities, Dublin and Continental Europe. Helping our people to grow and develop their careers is vitally important to us and is one of our highest priorities. We have many exciting and unique internal development programmes, each crafted to make sure our employees get the learning they need to be able to take the next step in their careers. Dalata Hotel Group is an Inclusive Employer. We actively encourage people from all backgrounds and abilities to apply to be part of our team. However, we understand everyone's needs are different; therefore, if you require any accommodations with this application process, please highlight your needs when you are completing your application process.

title: Apple Sales Team Member - Part-time
description: **SELECT** **Dundrum** **Part\-time role (Minimum 20 hours per week)** **€14\.15 per hour plus performance related commission** **Select** (part of the Select Technology Group) is the leading **Apple Premium Reseller** in the UK and Ireland . We are looking for a dynamic **part\-time** salesperson in our Dundrum store. Showcasing the full Apple product line in a customer friendly environment, Select is renowned for providing outstanding customer service. Whether you are a consumer or professional customer, you will enjoy friendly, knowledgeable advice and support at a store conveniently located near you. The training you will receive will give you the enviable position of being the first to hear about new Apple products and initiatives. You will be there to provide the excitement about the products which will naturally drive you to share your knowledge with your team members and customers alike. You will be joining a Team which is extremely driven to not only meet but exceed all sales and service performance goals, all whilst demonstrating your personal ethos of 'no limits'. Above all else you are there to ensure that every customer receives the best experience of shopping with Select from the minute they walk into your store. Your role will be diverse and not just restricted to customer interaction. As an ambitious individual you will welcome opportunities to gain knowledge regarding visual merchandising, product and service knowledge, as well as opportunities to earn and improve sales techniques. With opportunities to further your career, your success will not only be measured by individual productivity and overall store performance, but also by your attitude and attention to detail. What do you get from working at **Select**? * A growing love and passion for all things Apple * A great sense of achievement and empowerment for helping our customers discover which Mac, iPhone, Watch or iPad is right for them this is our passion * Competitive salary with commission * Pension Scheme * Fantastic Employee discounts * Employee Incentives * Employee Assistance Programme (EAP) **If this sounds like a place you would like to work, you are aged 18 or over, have the Right To Work in Ireland, and you can demonstrate your passion, knowledge and determination, then we would like to hear from you.** **Please note that we are hiring for a part\-time role.** **To apply for the role of Store Team Member, please submit your CV.** **IND1**

title: Artificial Intelligence Engineer
description: Our Dublin based client are looking for a **Lead Engineer, AI** to join their team on a permanent basis For this role they are looking for an individual who has experience in **AI** as it's a major part of the role and journey this company are on You will be part of a growing team and be the subject matter expert for AI Development in Dublin The position is with a well established company who are a market leader in their sector. **Responsibilities:** * Define and implement the AI architecture and standards * Lead on the overall AI strategy, roadmap * Research the relevant AI tools to solve specific business problems * Lead on the proof\-of\-concept (PoC) trials for new technologies and manage relationships with AI vendors and consultants. * Design and deliver AI, automation, and data solutions across various departments in the company. Cross collaboration and engagement is a key skill here * Integrate AI capabilities into ERP, APIs, and enterprise platforms * Establish ethical, secure, and GDPR‑compliant AI guardrails * Leadership in a hands\-on and highly technical role * Manage and mentor a team and help to hire **Requirements:** * 7\+ years of software development or architecture experience, with at least 3\-4 years in senior or management level roles * Have significant experience architecting and building enterprise\-wide cloud\-native solutions. * Programming skills and a background in Python * Ideally have exposure to projects for Digital Transformation * Gen AI and Intelligent systems that include LLM, RAG and Agentic AI * Hands on experience with Gen AI systems would be an advantage * Excellent understanding of software development best practices, design patterns, and SDLC methodologies. **Desirable** *:* * Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field. * Experience with CI/CD, infrastructure as code, and cloud ecosystems. * Understanding of data governance, security architecture, and compliance in cloud environments. **HOW TO APPLY:** To apply for this role, please submit your updated CV. If your skills and experience match job requirements, you will be contacted to discuss your application in detail. **SIMILAR ROLES OPEN:** As a specialized recruitment agency, we also have several other permanent and contract IT roles available. title: Asset Technician
description: Job Summary We are looking for an Asset Technician to support data center asset management activities, including asset receiving, labeling, inventory checks, movement tracking, documentation, and asset lifecycle support. This role is suitable for candidates who are detail\-oriented, reliable, and comfortable working in a structured operational environment. Previous experience in warehouse, logistics, IT asset management, data center asset control, or inventory management is preferred. Key Responsibilities * Support daily asset management activities in the data center, including receiving, labeling, registering, checking, and tracking IT equipment and related materials. * Maintain accurate asset records and ensure physical assets match system records. * Support asset movement, handover, storage, return, replacement, and disposal processes according to company and customer requirements. * Assist with regular inventory checks, reconciliation, and audit preparation. * Work with IT operations, facility, logistics, procurement, and customer\-side teams to ensure accurate asset flow. * Identify and report asset discrepancies, missing items, damaged equipment, or process deviations. * Maintain clean, organized, and secure storage areas for data center assets and spare parts. * Prepare basic reports and documentation related to asset status, inventory results, and operational activities. Requirements * Previous experience in asset management, inventory control, warehouse operations, logistics, data center support, or IT equipment handling is preferred. * Strong attention to detail and ability to follow procedures accurately. * Good Excel or spreadsheet skills. Experience with asset management systems, ticketing systems, or inventory tools is a plus. * Able to handle physical equipment and work in an on\-site data center environment. * Good communication skills and ability to coordinate with different teams. * Responsible, organized, and able to manage repetitive but accuracy\-critical tasks. * Basic understanding of IT equipment, servers, network devices, or data center hardware is an advantage. * English communication skills are required. Chinese language skills are a plus depending on project needs. What We Value * Strong safety awareness, compliance mindset, and respect for procedures. * Hands\-on attitude and willingness to work in an on\-site technical environment. * Reliability, punctuality, ownership, and attention to detail. * Clear communication and timely escalation when issues or risks appear. * Teamwork across IT operations, facility, security, asset management, vendors, and customer\-side teams. * A learning mindset. Candidates do not need to know everything from day one, but they must be able to follow SOPs carefully and grow with the project. title: Assistant Accountant
description: A growing and highly successful organisation is seeking an Assistant Accountant to join its finance team on a permanent basis. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated finance professional looking to gain broad exposure across financial reporting, month\-end accounting, budgeting, audit, and business operations within a dynamic and expanding business. Working as part of a collaborative finance team, you will play a key role in supporting the month\-end close process, maintaining financial controls, and delivering accurate financial reporting across the organisation. **Key Responsibilities Include** * Assisting with month\-end close activities including journals, accruals, prepayments, and reconciliations * Processing and reviewing Accounts Payable, Accounts Receivable, and banking transactions * Supporting management accounts preparation and financial reporting * Assisting with budgeting and forecasting processes * Performing variance analysis and investigating discrepancies * Supporting statutory audits and compliance requirements * Maintaining accurate financial records and strong audit trails * Contributing to finance process improvements, automation initiatives, and reporting enhancements **About You** To be successful in this role, you will have: * A minimum of 2 years' experience in a finance or accounting position * Experience with month\-end accounting, reconciliations, journals, accruals, and prepayments * Strong Excel skills including Pivot Tables, lookups, and data analysis * Excellent attention to detail and analytical ability * Strong communication skills and the ability to work with stakeholders across the business * A proactive mindset with a desire to learn and develop professionally What's on Offer? * Hybrid working model with 3 days working from home * Full sponsorship and support towards completing your professional accounting qualification (ACCA, ACA, CIMA, CPA or equivalent) * Pension contribution * Professional development and exam support * Employee wellbeing programme * Additional annual leave benefits * Flexible working arrangements * Opportunity to join a growing organisation with excellent long\-term career prospects This role would suit an Assistant Accountant or Trainee Accountant who is actively pursuing a professional qualification and is looking to accelerate their development within a supportive and forward\-thinking finance function. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Assistant Product Technologist
description: **Assistant Product Technologist – Electricals \& Accessories** My client, a leading international fashion retailer, is seeking an **Assistant Product Technologist – Electricals \& Accessories** to join their Quality Assurance team. This role supports the development of safe, compliant, and commercially sound products in line with Primark Quality Assurance Standards. Dublin\-based, permanent position. **Package** * Generous Salary DOE * Bonus * Pension * Health * Career Progression **Responsibilities of this Assistant Product Technologist – Electricals \& Accessories include:** * Support product development and ensure samples follow best practice processes and timelines. * Assess commercial quality standards and testing requirements across electrical and accessories categories. * Investigate sample rejections and quality issues, ensuring clear communication with Buying teams and suppliers. * Review and approve product specification sheets and instruction manuals, providing feedback where necessary. * Manage testing submissions and reporting by supplier, product area, and product type. * Compile and analyse tech packs and scorecards to support delivery of key line programmes and cost efficiencies. * Monitor returns reports and customer feedback to identify recurring issues and support corrective action plans. * Support risk assessment for pre\-shipment and CFS inspections with guidance from the Product Technologist. * Build competitor awareness to benchmark product quality and performance against key retailers. * Partner with Compliance teams to ensure quality standards and labelling requirements align with legal and industry standards, particularly for electrical products. * Liaise with suppliers on technical development, testing queries, and improvement plans. **Requirements of this Assistant Product Technologist – Electricals \& Accessories include:** * Minimum 5 years’ experience within Product Technology, QA, Compliance, or Product Testing in a retail environment. * Knowledge of electrical product standards, labelling requirements, and compliance processes. * Commercial awareness with the ability to balance quality, cost, and speed to market in a volume retail environment. * Highly organised with strong attention to detail and experience working with reports, testing submissions, and tech packs. title: BIM Detailer / Engineer - Leixlip - 144931
description: **Position: BIM Detailer / Engineer** **Location: Office –Leixlip** **Company Description:** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description:** As a BIM Detailer/Engineer, you will play a pivotal role in our project development and implementation process. You will work closely with the Project Team Leads as well as a multidisciplinary team of engineers, Construction team and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge projects. Current role is based in Leixlip, Co. Kildare. **Role and Responsibilities will include:** * Model Development: Create detailed 3D BIM models of mechanical systems, including HVAC, plumbing, and process via P\&ID schematics, using an array of Autodesk Products along with the latest Point Cloud Technology and full support methodology, ensuring accuracy and completeness. * Co\-ordination: Work within and experienced BIM team liaising with architects, structural engineers, and other project stakeholders to resolve design conflicts and ensure seamless integration of all systems within the BIM model. * Clash Detection: Utilise software like Navisworks Manage to identify and resolve clashes between mechanical systems and other building components before construction. * Documentation: Prepare 3D models to produce 2D design drawings, shop drawings, based on the accurate and fully constructable BIM models. * BIM Standards \& Compliance: Ensure that all modelling activities adhere to project\-specific BIM requirements, industry standards, and the project's BIM Execution Plan (BEP). * Technical Support: Provide technical support to junior members of the project team, assisting with the implementation and use of BIM technology along with attendance at required meetings. * Revisions \& Optimization: Manage revision and change to the models and conduct design analysis to optimize system performance and identify technical challenges. The successful candidate will be supported in developing their knowledge and skills as their responsibilities grow and evolve. **Skills and Requirements** : To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Technical Proficiency: Strong proficiency in BIM software, in particular AutoCAD, Plant 3D and Navisworks. * Mechanical Systems Knowledge: A solid understanding of mechanical engineering principles and systems, such as HVAC and piping. * Collaboration Skills: Excellent communication and collaboration skills to work effectively within a multidisciplinary team. * Problem\-Solving Abilities: Strong problem\-solving skills to identify and resolve technical challenges and design conflicts. * BIM Workflow Understanding: Knowledge of BIM workflows, content standards, and the ability to apply them to projects * Verbal and Written Communication: The ability to clearly explain complex technical information to both technical and non\-technical stakeholders is vital for project success. * Teamwork: Working effectively with diverse teams, including architects, structural engineers, and project managers, is fundamental in a collaborative BIM environment. * Interpersonal Skills: Strong interpersonal skills, including active listening and constructive conflict resolution, help foster positive working relationships. * Problem\-Solving \& Analysis: The ability to identify, analyse, and resolve complex issues within BIM models, especially those related to clashes and integration, is critical. * Attention to Detail: High precision in modelling, documentation, and coordination tasks prevents errors and ensures the quality of the final output. * Adaptability and Flexibility: The construction and BIM industries evolve rapidly, so engineers must be flexible to new technologies and fast\-paced environments. * Continuous Learning: A commitment to ongoing learning is essential to keep up with advanced BIM tools, software updates, and new methodologies. * Organization and Time Management: Effectively managing tasks and meeting project deadlines is crucial for delivering projects on time. * Project Management Support: Experience in supporting project management activities, including scheduling and cost estimation, is beneficial. **Benefits** We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note. We are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time.

description: **Senior \| Intermediate \| Junior** You will work on high\-quality projects as part of a collaborative and professional environment, contributing to the delivery of building services design from concept through to completion. **Key Responsibilities** * Deliver electrical/mechanical building services designs across all project stages * Design systems including power, lighting, heating, ventilation and associated services * Coordinate with multidisciplinary project teams * Support project delivery to a high technical standard * Engage with clients and stakeholders (senior level) * Mentor and support junior engineers (senior level) * Take increasing ownership of project elements (intermediate level) **Requirements** **Senior Engineers** * Degree in Electrical/Mechanical Building Services Engineering * Strong experience within an Irish building services consultancy * Proven project delivery and client\-facing experience * Strong technical and coordination skills * Ability to lead projects and mentor engineers **Intermediate Engineers** * 3–6 years’ experience in building services design * Consultancy experience * Strong technical and coordination capability * Motivated to progress to senior level **Junior Engineers** * Degree in Electrical/Mechanical Building Services Engineering * Strong interest in building services design * Good communication and teamwork skills * Willingness to learn and develop **Development \& Salary** **Junior Engineers** You will be supported in your professional development, with structured guidance and real project experience to help you build a long\-term career within PMEP. Salary will be competitive and reflective of your qualifications and potential, with clear progression as your experience develops. **Intermediate Engineers** You will be supported in your continued professional development, with increasing responsibility and project ownership to help you progress your career within PMEP. Salary will be competitive and reflective of your experience, technical capability and level of responsibility. **Senior Engineers** You will take ownership of project delivery and play a key role in maintaining technical standards, mentoring engineers and supporting the continued growth of the team, with opportunities to further progress your career within PMEP. Salary will be competitive and reflective of your experience, technical capability and level of responsibility.

title: Building Management Systems Engineer
description: **Junior BMS Engineer** Are you a technically minded graduate, junior engineer or electrician ready to kickstart your career in Building Management Systems (BMS)? An exciting opportunity has opened up for a Junior BMS Engineer to join a highly respected BMS integrator working on some of the most innovative commercial, pharmaceutical and mission\-critical projects across Ireland. **Dublin, Ireland** * €40,000 \- €45,000 * Yearly Bonus * Company Vehicle * Full Training and Development * Private Healthcare **Responsibilities:** * Assist in the design, commissioning and support of BMS systems across a variety of cutting\-edge projects. * Work closely with senior engineers and project managers to gain hands\-on experience with systems like Trend, Tridium or Cylon. * Get involved in both on\-site and office\-based work, no two days are the same. * Learn the full project lifecycle from specification and design, through to programming, installation and commissioning. **Experience Required:** * Degree or diploma in electrical engineering, building services, mechatronics or a related field. * Electrically Qualified. * A passion for automation, controls or energy\-efficient building systems * Excellent problem\-solving skills and eagerness to learn * Ideally some exposure to HVAC or BMS systems (through a placement, internship, or coursework). **What’s On Offer:** * Competitive salary and benefits package * Structured training and development plan * Opportunity to gain certifications and hands\-on experience with industry\-leading BMS technologies * Career progression with a clear pathway toward project engineering and senior roles * Collaborative team environment with supportive mentors For more information please contact: luke.alliband@clear\-er.com

description: **Who we are** Ardmac is an award\-winning international construction specialist that delivers complex high value workspaces and technical environments. services design engineer,** a key member of the engineering design department, responsible for the development and delivery of detailed design and construction information for the HVAC and building services systems. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Development of detailed design information for elements of the mechanical/HVAC systems for particular projects. * Responsible for ensuring that the mechanical/HVAC design is developed in line with relevant Client and industry standards and guidelines. * To provide technical support during the tendering process. * Developing relationships with customers, end user clients and supply chain * Market research relating to products/materials to be specified as part of the mechanical/HVAC systems being designed. * Ability to complete technical assessments on products and materials from various vendors to determine the optimum design solution for a particular project. * Ensuring co\-ordination of all design information with site construction teams * Knowledge generation of all relevant products including statutory and regulatory developments. * Attending tender launch and adjudication meetings as required. * Implementing technical innovation and value creation * Development of design solutions specific to Ardmac’s modular and off\-site manufacturing offerings **What We’re Looking For** Qualifications: * BSc. in Building Services Engineering or Mechanical Engineering * A minimum of 5 years’ experience of designing mechanical/HVAC services installations in high tech industrial sectors such as cleanroom, data centres and medtech. **Ability and knowledge of:** * Statutory and Regulatory Procedures * Building Services best practice * Detailed knowledge of Technical Systems and Procedures * Knowledge \& experience of developing design for projects in Cleanroom facilities. * Knowledge and experience of modular/ off\-site design solutions * Understanding of best practice for design of mechanical/HVAC systems for Cleanroom facilities. * BIM using revit MEP * Experience in the use of relevant MEP design software packages. * Standard forms of contract, inc Design and Build * Estimating, procurement \& payment terms Candidates must already possess authorization to work in Ireland without the need for a work permit application. **Why Join Us** * Work at the forefront of high\-specification construction and technical environments and contribute to projects that support critical industries such as pharmaceutical manufacturing and global data infrastructure * Be part of a collaborative, delivery\-focused organisation with a strong reputation for quality * Competitive salary and benefits package * Work – life balance with hybrid working available in most roles * Training and Development Opportunities **Ready to Apply?** If you have the experience detailed above in building services design, we’d welcome your application. Apply now to be part of a team delivering excellence in cleanrooms, life sciences, and data centre environments.

title: Business Process & Automation Intern
description: This role has been designed as ‘Hybrid’ with an expectation that you will work on average 2 days per week from an HPE office. **Who We Are** Hewlett Packard Enterprise is the global edge\-to\-cloud company advancing the way people live and work. We help companies connect, protect, analyze, and act on their data and applications wherever they live, from edge to cloud, so they can turn insights into outcomes at the speed required to thrive in today’s complex world. You’ll be immersed in a high\-impact, collaborative environment that bridges strategic process design, intelligent automation, and business value realization. As part of our **Process \& Operations Transformation (POT)** organization, you’ll be exposed to diverse initiatives that span process mapping and engineering, automation, internal stakeholder support, and strategic problem\-solving and have the chance to focus on a particular area to make an impact. You’ll work alongside process owners, automation analysts, and transformation leads who are driving change across our business. This is a dynamic opportunity for students who want to build practical experience in business process management, data\-driven decision\-making, and digital execution. **Start Date: 04\. July 2026 or later** **Location: Dublin Ireland (Hybrid)** **Mandatory: You must be enrolled at a university throughout the duration of the internship** **What You’ll Do** As a member of the POT internship program, you may: * Support process engineering initiatives by helping build process maps, identifying inefficiencies, and observing real\-world workflow challenges. * Assist process owners and continuous improvement leads with documentation, stakeholder engagement, and change management tasks. * Participate in automation and analytics projects, where you may help gather requirements for RPA, explore business use cases for AI tools, or support dashboard/report design. * Contribute to value realization and initiative tracking, helping measure project outcomes and business impact. * Be part of ad hoc focused support teams for urgent transactional bottlenecks, problem analysis, or team deep dives into operational pain points. * Collaborate with transformation leads to prepare materials, stakeholder summaries, or project plans related to cross\-functional priorities. * Gain exposure to industry tools and techniques like Lean Six Sigma, process mining, and agile work management. **What You’ll Need** * Actively pursuing a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Business, Industrial Engineering, Operations Management, Data Analytics, or a related discipline. * Strong analytical, communication, and problem\-solving skills. * Enthusiasm for business process improvement, digital tools, and real\-world operational challenges. * Ability to manage your time, balance competing priorities, and thrive in a dynamic work environment. * Curiosity, a learning mindset, and interest in working with a diverse, globally distributed team. **What You’ll Gain** * Hands\-on experience contributing to enterprise process transformation projects. * Exposure to real business challenges and the opportunity to help shape how work gets done. * 1:1 mentorship and the ability to shadow process, automation, and transformation experts. * A strong foundation in how modern companies use technology and data to simplify and improve operations. **About Our Team** The **Process \& Operations Transformation (POT)** team plays a central role in how HPEFS drives simplification, standardization, and intelligent automation across our global operations. Interns become part of a community focused on continuous improvement, with clear exposure to both business strategy and digital execution. **At HPE, We’re** * Human Rights Campaign’s 2023\-2024 Corporate Equality Index * Great Place to Work’s Best Workplace for Parents 2023 * Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® 2024 * Ethisphere World’s Most Ethical Company 2024 * The Civic 50 Honoree * JUST Jobs Leader 2024 * Forbes 2024 America’s Best Employers For Diversity * America's Most Just Companies 2024 * Fortune Best Workplaces in Texas™ 2024 * Bay Area's Best Places to Work 2024 **Ready to take the next step?** Open up opportunities with HPE. **Additional Skills** **What We Can Offer You:** **Health \& Wellbeing** We strive to provide our team members and their loved ones with a comprehensive suite of benefits that supports their physical, financial and emotional wellbeing. **Personal \& Professional Development** We also invest in your career because the better you are, the better we all are. We have specific programs catered to helping you reach any career goals you have — whether you want to become a knowledge expert in your field or apply your skills to another division. **Unconditional Inclusion** We are unconditionally inclusive in the way we work and celebrate individual uniqueness. We know varied backgrounds are valued and succeed here. We have the flexibility to manage our work and personal needs. We make bold moves, together, and are a force for good. **Let's Stay Connected** Follow @HPECareers on Instagram to see the latest on people, culture and tech at HPE. \#ireland \#hpefs, \#interns **Job** Administration **Job Level** N/A HPE is an Equal Employment Opportunity/ Veterans/Disabled/LGBT employer. title: Chef de Partie

title: Chemistry Specialist (Masters/PhDs)
description: **Chemistry Specialist (Masters/PhDs) \- AI Training** **About The Role** What if your years of chemistry expertise could directly shape how AI reasons through the most complex scientific problems in the world? We're looking for Chemistry Specialists with advanced degrees to design, solve, and evaluate graduate\-level chemistry problems that train cutting\-edge AI models to think like expert scientists. This is a fully remote, flexible contract role built for working chemists and researchers who want to apply their deep domain knowledge in a high\-impact, forward\-looking way. No AI background required — just serious chemistry expertise and a commitment to scientific rigor. * Organization: Alignerr * Type: Hourly Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Design and solve complex, real\-world chemistry problems spanning organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Review and evaluate AI\-generated chemistry content for scientific accuracy, depth, and clarity * Craft challenging problem statements that push AI reasoning to graduate\-level standards * Collaborate asynchronously with AI researchers and fellow domain experts to improve model performance * Ensure all deliverables meet high standards of scientific rigor and precision * Work independently on task\-based assignments — fully on your own schedule **Who You Are** * Master's or PhD in Chemistry or a closely related field * Deep expertise in at least one of: organic, inorganic, physical, or computational chemistry * Comfortable working with technical concepts at a graduate or research level * Naturally detail\-oriented with strong written communication skills in English * Self\-motivated and reliable when working independently * Experience coding in Python or using computational chemistry tools (e.g., Gaussian, VASP) is a strong plus **Nice to Have** * Prior experience with data annotation, content evaluation, or quality assurance workflows * Familiarity with AI tools or language model evaluation as an end user * Background in academic research, scientific publishing, or curriculum development * Experience designing problem sets, exams, or instructional materials at a graduate level **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects alongside the world's leading research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work when and where it suits you * Apply your advanced chemistry knowledge in a meaningful, intellectually stimulating way * Freelance autonomy with the structure of impactful, task\-based work * Contribute to AI systems that will shape how science is understood and communicated at scale * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension as new projects launch

title: Civil Engineer - Water Networks
description: **The Opportunity** **THE OPPORTUNITY** This is an opportunity to join Stantec’s growing water sector team in Ireland, where you will contribute to the delivery of critical infrastructure that supports communities, protects the environment, and addresses the challenges of climate change. As a Civil Engineer, you will work on a diverse range of water and wastewater projects, from concept and feasibility through to detailed design and construction support, gaining exposure across the full project lifecycle. You will be part of a collaborative, multidisciplinary environment, working alongside experts in engineering, environmental science, and planning to deliver integrated and sustainable solutions. The role offers the chance to build your technical expertise in areas such as network design, treatment processes, and asset management, while also developing your project management and client\-facing skills **About The Role** As a Civil Engineer in Stantec’s water team, you will support the delivery of water and wastewater projects across all stages, from concept to construction. You will contribute to technical design work, including drawings, calculations, and specifications, while assisting with reports and contract documentation. Working within a multidisciplinary team, you will collaborate with engineers, environmental specialists, and project managers, gaining broad project experience and exposure to clients and stakeholders. This role offers a strong platform to develop your technical skills and grow your career within Ireland’s water sector. **YOUR IMPACT** In this role, you will contribute to the delivery of essential water and wastewater infrastructure that supports communities, public health, and environmental protection across Ireland. Your work will help ensure reliable water services, improve network performance, and enhance resilience to climate change. By applying sound engineering principles and attention to detail, you will support the development of practical, efficient, and sustainable design solutions. Your input will help projects meet regulatory requirements, reduce environmental impact, and deliver long\-term value for clients and stakeholders. Through collaboration with multidisciplinary teams, you will play a part in driving high\-quality project outcomes, strengthening client relationships, and supporting the continued growth and reputation of Stantec in Ireland’s water sector. **About You** You are a qualified Civil Engineer with a strong technical foundation and proven experience in water network design. You bring a practical, solutions\-focused mindset and are comfortable working both independently and as part of a collaborative, multidisciplinary team. **You Will Demonstrate** * A minimum Level 8 degree in Engineering (or a related discipline) * Strong post\-graduate experience in water network design * Solid professional and technical knowledge, with the ability to apply engineering principles effectively * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage with colleagues and clients **WHY JOIN US** * We put people first: We're proud of our friendly, inclusive and collaborative environment where everyone feels valued and driven to succeed. * We are driven to achieve: Named in the top 50 companies to work by Glassdoor 2025\. Recognised by Corporate Knights as one of the world's top 10 most sustainable companies 2025\. \= * We do what is right: Industry leading training and development as well as paid for professional subscriptions. A professional progression plan, supporting you to become chartered. * We are better together: A supportive and collaborative team environment to assist with the growth of your career. * Growth: We're on an exciting growth journey across the Ireland \- we want you to be part of it! * Great Benefits: Competitive salary, enhanced pension plan, annual leave with the option to buy/sell additional days, private medical insurance, group income protection \& life assurance included as standard! **About Stantec** Whether you’re starting out on your engineering career or are looking for a fresh challenge, Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, is a great career move. Based in Dublin, Galway, Castlebar and Cork, we are actively recruiting for team players with experience in any of the following areas – water and wastewater treatment process and network design, integrated network management, structural design, site development, road and bridge engineering, project management and administration. You’ll be working for a dynamic company with an excellent reputation in the industry. Ryan Hanley, now part of Stantec, offers a great working environment amongst friendly professionals with an emphasis on teamwork. It’s a company where your ideas and opinions will be valued and your efforts recognised and rewarded. We have been awarded the silver Investors in Diversity accreditation by the Irish Centre for Diversity for our commitment to quality, diversity and inclusion (EDI) in the workplace.

title: Clinical SDTM Programmer
description: Clinical SDTM Programmer (advanced) ICON is a global healthcare intelligence and clinical research organisation united by a mission to bring new medicines and treatments to patients faster. As a values\-driven organisation, integrity, collaboration, agility, and inclusion are at the heart of how we work and interact with each other, customers, patients and suppliers. We are recruiting an Advanced Clinical Programmer (SDTM) to support a long‑term FSP partnership with a global, top‑tier pharmaceutical company. This role is highly focused on SDTM oversight, standards, and submission readiness, with direct visibility across study teams and regulatory deliverables. About the Role As an Advanced SDTM Clinical Programmer, you will provide end‑to‑end oversight of SDTM programming activities, from study set‑up through to submission‑ready packages. You will act as a technical authority on CDISC standards, ensuring quality, compliance, and consistency across trials while collaborating closely with Data Management, Statistics, and clinical teams. This is an ideal opportunity for someone who enjoys SDTM governance, review, and strategic input, not just hands‑on programming. Key Responsibilities * Provide expert oversight of SDTM programming activities from study start‑up through database lock and regulatory submission * Lead or review the development of submission‑ready CDISC SDTM packages, including aCRFs, define.xml, and cSDRGs * Review and contribute to trial set‑up documentation, including data transfer agreements, metadata, and trial design specifications * Create or review SDTM mapping specifications for internal and external data sources * Generate or oversee the creation of SDTM datasets using standardized conversion frameworks and utilities * Ensure high\-quality, compliant datasets, including Pinnacle 21 review, readiness for interim analyses, medical review, and final lock * Program or oversee data quality checks and review reports to support Data Management and study teams * Maintain regulatory awareness, ensuring SDTM deliverables meet FDA, PMDA, and global submission requirements * Act as a subject matter expert across CDISC standards (SDTM, CDASH, controlled terminology, define.xml) * Support the development and implementation of programming standards within the organisation * At senior level: mentor junior or flexible resources and provide leadership or coordination of support teams where required Key Interfaces You will work closely with a wide range of stakeholders, including: * Clinical Programmers and Statistical Programmers * Clinical Trial and Data Management teams * Clinical Data Standards groups * External DM CRO partners * Enabling Business \& Information Services (EBIS) Required Experience \& Skills * Bachelor’s degree (or higher) in Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics, or a related scientific discipline (or equivalent experience) * Strong clinical programming background with hands‑on trial experience * Expert knowledge of SAS programming within a clinical research environment * Advanced expertise in CDISC SDTM, including aCRFs, define.xml, and cSDRGs * Deep understanding of clinical data structures (SDTM and working knowledge of ADaM) * Experience working in global, cross‑functional clinical research teams * Comfortable operating in a fast‑paced, matrixed FSP environment * Strong organisational, communication, and stakeholder management skills * Project oversight or coordination experience is highly desirable Why Apply? * Long‑term stability through a strategic FSP partnership * Direct exposure to high‑impact regulatory submissions * Opportunity to operate as an SDTM standards and oversight expert, not just a producer * Collaborative, global environment with access to industry‑leading tools and standards * Clear pathway for senior technical influence and mentorship Employment with ICON is contingent upon having the legal right to work in the country where the role is based. **Rewards \& Benefits** ICON offers a competitive and comprehensive total rewards package designed to support your health, wellbeing, and career development. Benefits may include: * Competitive base salary and performance related incentives * Health and wellbeing programmes including medical, dental, and vision coverage where applicable * Retirement and pension plans * Life assurance and disability coverage * Employee assistance programmes and wellbeing resources * Learning and development opportunities through structured training and career pathways Benefits may vary depending on role and location. Visit our careers site to read more about the benefits ICON offers. **Inclusion and Accessibility** ICON is an equal opportunity employer. We are committed to building an inclusive and accessible workplace where everyone feels valued and supported. If you require reasonable accommodations during the recruitment process, please let us know or submit a request **here**.

title: Cloud Security Analyst (Daily Rate)

title: Compliance Analyst
description: A leading financial services organisation in Dublin is seeking a Compliance Analyst to join their dedicated Compliance Department. * Join a well\-established financial services organisation in Dublin with a strong reputation for nurturing talent and providing comprehensive training opportunities. * Play a key role in maintaining regulatory standards by conducting email reviews, supporting investigations, and enhancing compliance policies within a supportive team environment. **What You'll Do** * Conduct thorough reviews of email communications for trading groups, ensuring all correspondence adheres to regulatory requirements and internal standards. * Perform detailed searches related to internal investigations, collaborating closely with colleagues across departments to gather relevant information. * Prepare comprehensive weekly and monthly memos summarising findings from email surveillance reviews, presenting clear insights to senior management. * Assist in the development and enhancement of compliance policies and procedures, adapting them to reflect changes in business operations, regulations, and emerging issues. * Stay up\-to\-date with relevant regulatory developments and changes, proactively sharing knowledge with the wider team to ensure collective understanding. * Participate actively in other compliance\-related projects as needed, demonstrating flexibility and willingness to support organisational goals. * Work collaboratively with colleagues across departments to build effective working relationships that facilitate smooth project delivery. * Utilise technological applications and tools efficiently to streamline compliance processes and improve overall department productivity. * Contribute positively to a culture of continuous improvement by identifying areas for process optimisation within the compliance function. **What You Bring** * Bachelor’s degree (preferably in Business, Finance, Economics or related field) with at least three years’ experience in compliance roles within financial services. * Prior experience at a Registered Investment Adviser or Broker\-Dealer is highly desirable for this position. * Strong understanding of regulatory agencies such as Central Bank of Ireland, FCA, SEC, CFTC, MAS along with familiarity with key regulations including Investment Advisers Act 1940, Securities Act 1933, Financial Services and Markets Act 2000 (FSMA). * Excellent team player who thrives on co\-operation and collaboration within diverse groups. * Ability to prioritise tasks effectively in environments characterised by constant change and evolving demands. * Entrepreneurial mindset combined with proven ability to manage projects independently while remaining responsive to team needs. * Exceptional writing skills paired with analytical abilities that enable you to produce clear reports and solve complex problems. * Proficiency with Microsoft Excel, Word, PowerPoint as well as other technological applications relevant to compliance work. * Broad knowledge of financial products and the wider financial services industry is considered advantageous for this role. * Strong interpersonal skills enabling you to build dependable relationships across departments. Robert Walters Operations Limited is an employment business and employment agency and welcomes applications from all candidates

title: Corporate and M&A Lawyer
