title: Creative Designer
description: At Real Nation, we create experiences and campaigns that engage, educate, and inspire change. Combining strategic thinking with creative execution, we deliver experiential campaigns, educational programmes, live and virtual events, and innovative brand experiences for a wide range of audiences. Our work is insight\-driven, inclusive, and focused on creating meaningful connections through impactful storytelling and immersive design. From large\-scale public events to bespoke brand activations, we craft experiences that resonate with people in memorable and authentic ways. We are looking for an experienced **Senior 3D Creative Designer** to join our creative team. This is a senior role for someone who can lead the creative development of live experiences, brand activations, exhibitions, corporate events, and immersive environments from initial concept through to final delivery. The ideal candidate will combine strong creative thinking with excellent 3D design, visual communication, and build awareness. They will be confident developing ideas, creating high\-quality visualisations, presenting to clients, and working closely with internal teams, suppliers, and production partners to bring creative concepts to life. This role requires someone who can think conceptually, design practically, and take ownership of high\-level creative projects across the experiential and events space. **Responsibilities** * Lead the creative development of experiential, event, activation, exhibition, and branded environment projects from brief through to delivery. * Lead the creative response to client briefs, shaping the overall concept, visual direction, spatial approach, and key experience moments. * Develop strong creative concepts, spatial ideas, moodboards, iterative options, 3D visuals, and client\-ready presentations. * Create high\-quality 3D designs, renders, layouts, and visualisations that clearly communicate the creative vision. * Direct (graphics and Art) the look and feel of projects across spatial design, branding, graphics, signage, environmental design, and built outputs. * Ensure creative ideas are commercially and practically deliverable, working closely with our events team in line with project builds/project teams to balance ambition, budget, timelines, and build requirements. * Work directly with clients to understand briefs, present concepts, respond to feedback, and manage creative expectations. * Collaborate with project managers, producers, designers, production teams, suppliers, contractors, and fabrication partners to ensure ideas are practical, achievable, and delivered to a high standard. * Prepare or oversee print\-ready artwork, design files, and visual assets for print, fabrication, digital display, and event delivery. * Oversee the quality and consistency of creative output across pitches, presentations, 3D visuals, artwork, production drawings, and final event delivery. * Attend site visits, surveys, installations, and live event delivery where required. * Support pitches, new business opportunities, and internal creative development sessions. * Provide creative guidance to junior or mid\-level designers across our teams where needed. * Stay informed on experiential design trends, materials, technologies, and creative installation approaches, bringing fresh thinking and innovation into client proposals and live projects. **Skills \& Requirements** * Minimum 6 – 10 year’s experience in experiential design, event design, spatial design, 3D design, art direction, production design, or a creative agency environment * .Strong portfolio showing experience across live events, brand activations, exhibitions, branded environments, stages, sets, installations, or immersive spaces * .Advanced 3D design and visualisation skills using software such as SketchUp, Cinema 4D, Blender, Rhino, 3ds Max, V\-Ray, or similar * .Strong Adobe Creative Cloud skills, especially Illustrator, Photoshop, and InDesign * .Confident creating polished pitch decks with powerpoint/indesign, client presentations, moodboards, layouts, and visuals * .Strong understanding of spatial design, environmental graphics, brand application, materials, fabrication, print production, and event build processes * .Experience working directly with clients and presenting creative ideas clearly and confidently * .Ability to manage multiple projects, work under pressure, meet deadlines, and maintain a high standard of creative output * .Strong communication, collaboration, and problem\-solving skills * .Flexible and adaptable, with an understanding that occasional out\-of\-hours work may be required due to the nature of live events and installations . **What We Offe** * r * A creative culture that values curiosity, innovation, strong ideas, and high\-quality desig * n.The opportunity to work across a wide range of projects, including brand activations, live events, exhibitions, education programmes, behavioural science initiatives, experiential campaigns, and corporate learning experience * s.The chance to collaborate with some of Ireland’s leading brand * s.A supportive, cross\-functional team environment within a growing compan * y.The opportunity to shape creative ideas from concept through to live deliver * y.Hybrid and flexible working arrangement * s.Full\-time positio * n.Salary dependent on experience (Range: DO E)

title: Custody Client Services Analyst
description: **Title: Custody Client Services Analyst** **Duration: 12 Months Contract** **Location: Dublin, Ireland 🇮🇪(Hybrid)** We are currently working with a leading financial services organization seeking a Custody Client Services Analyst to join their growing Custody team. This opportunity is ideal for professionals with experience in securitie *s s* ervices, custody operations, client onboarding, KYC/AML, or billing functions who are looking to further develop their career within a dynamic and client\-focused environment. **Key Responsibilities:** * Manage the end\-to\-end onboarding process for new custody clients. * Coordinate the collection and review of KYC, AML, legal, and compliance documentation. * Act as a key point of contact for clients throughout the onboarding journey. * Support monthly client billing activities, including fee calculations and out\-of\-pocket expense management. * Investigate and resolve billing queries and discrepancies. * Maintain accurate client records and operational documentation. * Collaborate with Operations, Finance, Compliance, and Client Service teams. * Contribute to process improvement initiatives and operational excellence. **What We're Looking For:** * 2–5\+ years of experience within Financial Services Operations, Securities Services, Asset Management, or Custody. * Experience in Client Onboarding, KYC/AML, Client Services, Billing, or Accounts Receivable. * Understanding of the securities post\-trade lifecycle and custody operations. * Strong stakeholder management and communication skills. * Excellent attention to detail and problem\-solving abilities. * Advanced Microsoft Excel skills are highly desirable. **Nice to Have:** * Experience working within a global custodian or asset servicing environment. * Knowledge of settlement, safekeeping, and asset servicing processes. * Exposure to UK market operations and regulatory requirements.

title: Customer Care Specialist I
description: **Want to help us help others? We’re hiring!** GoFundMe is the world’s most powerful community for good, dedicated to helping people help each other. By uniting individuals and nonprofits in one place, GoFundMe makes it easy and safe for people to ask for help and support causes—for themselves and each other. Together, our community has raised more than $40 billion since 2010\. Join us! The GoFundMe team is searching for our next Customer Care Specialist based in Dublin, Ireland, to effectively resolve customer support requests \& drive continuous improvement for the GoFundMe experience. **The Job** * Making a difference by helping customers from all around the world, who are raising or giving money for various causes important to them, all through some of the most exciting \& tough moments of their lives. * Providing exceptional \& human customer support via live messaging \& email, with the potential to support phone calls, for a wide variety of complex support needs \& escalations, including helping customers troubleshoot bank transfer verification, navigate sensitive social dynamics related to fundraising, \& optimise their success through effective use of the GoFundMe product. * Passionately championing customer feedback to improve the GoFundMe experience. * Occasionally support the team with ad\-hoc projects or tasks directly tied to the team’s vision \& annual objectives. * Working in a dynamic environment, hybrid based, with a willingness to work from the office a few times a month. **You** * Metric \& goal driven \- You enjoy challenging yourself \& holding yourself accountable to reaching targets! We set high standards so we can provide a human \& effortless experience to our customers. * Customer centric \- You care about the experience our customers have \& understand the impact you have on their GoFundMe experience. * Compassionate \- You truly care about people \& want to help them, using strong emotional intelligence to adjust your approach based on each customer’s situation. * Adaptable \- You enjoy being part of a growing organization. You thrive under new challenges \& in an ever\-evolving environment! * Effective in communication \- You are a strong communicator who is able to confidently use communication (written \& verbal) as a tool to address our customers’ needs \& work with teammates internally. * Tech\-savvy \- You are comfortable working in an online space, learning new tools quickly, \& troubleshooting tech\-issues in real time. * Critical thinker \& solution minded \- You seek out potential challenges \& trends, \& productively propose solutions. Everyone owns the success of our team! * Attentive to detial \- Did you notice "detail" is misspelled? * Resourceful \- You take initiative to research independently, organise your resources effectively, \& discover creative solutions to challenges you may encounter. * Invested in personal growth \- You have a growth mindset, stay hungry for feedback, \& have a strong desire to learn. * Team centered \- You enjoy empowering others, celebrating their wins, \& supporting others’ growth. * Leadership prone \- You proactively take initiative with the big picture in mind, \& seek opportunities to drive your team forward in the everyday **Preferred** * Fluency in German or French preferred but not required **Why you’ll love it here** * Make an Impact: Be part of a mission\-driven organization making a positive difference in millions of lives every year. * Innovative Environment: Work with a diverse, passionate, and talented team in a fast\-paced, forward\-thinking atmosphere. * Collaborative Team: Join a fun and collaborative team that works hard and celebrates success together. * Competitive Benefits: Enjoy competitive pay and comprehensive healthcare benefits. * Holistic Support: Enjoy financial assistance for things like hybrid work, family planning, along with generous parental leave, flexible time\-off policies, and mental health and wellness resources to support your overall well\-being. * Growth Opportunities: Participate in learning, development, and recognition programs to help you thrive and grow. * Commitment to DEI: Contribute to diversity, equity, and inclusion through ongoing initiatives and employee resource groups. * Community Engagement: Make a difference through our volunteering program. **We live by our core values:** *impatient to be great* , *find a way* , *earn trust every day* , *fueled by purpose* . Be a part of something bigger with us! The salary range for this position is €37,000\.00 \- €55,000\.00, plus equity and benefits in Ireland. The salary range was determined objective, gender\-neutral criteria, including work location, role, and level. Individual pay is determined by work location and additional factors including job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. Your recruiter can share more about the specific total compensation package based on your location during the hiring process. GoFundMe is proud to be an equal opportunity employer that actively pursues candidates of diverse backgrounds and experiences. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, ethnicity, nationality or national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, pregnancy status, marital status, age, medical condition, mental or physical disability, or military or veteran status. If you require a reasonable accommodation to complete a job application or a job interview or to otherwise participate in the hiring process, please contact us at accommodationrequests@gofundme.com. **Global Data Privacy Notice For Job Candidates And Applicants** Depending on your location, the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) or certain US privacy laws may regulate the way we manage the data of job applicants. Our full notice outlining how data will be processed as part of the application procedure for applicable locations is available here. By submitting your application, you are agreeing to our use and processing of your data as required. **Learn More About GoFundMe** We’re proud to partner with GoFundMe.org, an independent public charity, to extend the reach and impact of our generous community, while helping drive critical social change. You can learn more about GoFundMe.org’s activities and impact in their FY ‘25 annual report. Our annual “Year in Help” report reflects our community’s impact in advancing our mission of helping people help each other. For recent company news and announcements, visit our Newsroom.

title: Customer Support Representative
description: **Employment setup** The legal employer for this position is Arabian Private Holdings, a firm based in Saudi Arabia. This is a fully remote role open to candidates in several regions, including those familiar with the Ireland market context due to our current project activities. Candidates must already possess the right to work in their country of residence as we do not provide visa sponsorship. This is a full\-time position requiring a consistent weekly schedule. We require four hours of overlap with Riyadh time: 09:00 to 13:00: for team coordination, with the remainder of the day structured around the needs of our European clients. Compensation is competitive and paid monthly. You will report directly to a firm Principal. The hiring process consists of an initial profile review, a brief written technical assessment, and a final interview via video call. **About the role** This position acts as the primary support link for a scaling project with a leading European financial technology group. Our firm is currently assisting this client with their operational entry into Ireland, requiring a dedicated hand to manage user inquiries during this expansion. The role exists to ensure that technical and account\-related questions are met with the same analytical rigor we apply to our broader consulting mandates. **About Arabian Private Holdings** Arabian Private Holdings was founded on the principle that critical business problems do not require large consulting teams or hundred\-page slide decks. We work with family groups, listed corporations, and government entities to provide portfolio clarity and help refine their operating models. Our firm is principal\-led, meaning the senior professionals who take on a mandate are the same ones doing the work. We operate as a remote organization, valuing the quality of our analysis and the strength of our relationships over traditional office presence. We prefer direct communication and focus on delivering specific, defensible results for a limited number of clients. **What you will do** * Manage incoming inquiries from users of the financial technology platform, ensuring every response is accurate and clear. * Perform research into account discrepancies and technical issues to form a view on the necessary solution before responding. * Maintain a detailed log of recurring user problems to help the team identify patterns in the software operating model. * Draft and update support documentation to reflect the specific requirements and language used in the Ireland market. * Collaborate with firm Principals to ensure that support activities align with the client governance standards. * Submit weekly reports on response times and resolution quality for ongoing mandates. * Identify potential improvements in how we handle data and user privacy within the support workflow. **What we are looking for** * A high standard of written precision and the ability to explain complex financial features in plain language. * Familiarity with digital help desk tools and common spreadsheet applications for tracking data. * Comfortable working independently in a remote setting while maintaining a consistent output. * Logical rigor when troubleshooting technical problems or account issues. * Some exposure to financial services or a general interest in how technology affects corporate strategy. * A background in a service\-oriented environment where detail and accuracy are more important than speed alone. **How we work** We do not use complicated hierarchies or unnecessary meetings. Our remote operating model relies on clear written communication and individual accountability. Every member of the firm is expected to contribute to the clarity of our work, whether that is a support ticket or a portfolio review. Mentoring happens through direct collaboration on active mandates, where junior staff see how Principals form views and defend them. We prioritize results and logical consistency over traditional corporate rituals, and we expect our team to be direct about what they need to get the job done. This role is a fixed\-term engagement intended to support the aforementioned fintech expansion, with the possibility of project renewal based on performance and client needs. **What the role offers** * Regular interaction with firm Principals and exposure to high\-level corporate strategy work. * The opportunity to play a part in a significant European financial technology rollout. * A work environment that values logical thought and clear writing over buzzwords. * Flexible remote working conditions with a focus on defined professional outcomes. * Professional development in the areas of corporate governance and client service management.

title: Customs Clerk
description: **Company overview** Established in 2001 with head office in Dublin, A\&D Freight operates a full supply chain service across all industry sectors, covering all modes of transport. This role is ideal for someone looking to start a career in logistics and customs administration. Full training will be provided. The successful candidate will support customs processing and administration activities to ensure shipments move efficiently and accurately. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist with preparation and processing of customs documentation * Enter shipment and customs information into internal systems * Support the completion of import and export declarations * Review documentation for accuracy and completeness * Communicate with customers, drivers, agents and customs authorities where required * Maintain shipment records and filing systems * Monitor shipment progress and assist in resolving issues * Ensure compliance with company procedures and customs requirements * Carry out general administration duties as required **Reports To:** Operations Manager **Skills \& Experience Required** **Essential** * Good computer skills, including Microsoft Outlook and Excel * Strong attention to detail * Good communication skills * Organised and able to manage multiple tasks * Positive attitude and willingness to learn * Some flexibility may be required in the role and will be compensated accordingly **Desirable (not essential)** * Previous office administration experience * Experience using Thyme\-IT or TSS systems Customs, Logistics or transport experience * **Personal Attributes** * Reliable and punctual * Team player * Able to work under pressure Eager to learn and develop * **What We Offer** * Full training provided * 1\-day remote working per week * Company Laptop * Opportunity to develop within the logistics industry * Supportive team environment

title: Data Analyst
description: **Urgent Hire: Data Analyst** • Start date: ASAP • Duration: 12 months • Location: 1 week on site, Dublin \- 3 weeks working from home • Rate: €200\- €250 per day **Description:** * Bachelor’s Degree in a related field or equivalent practical experience * Experience working as a Data Analyst (typically 1–2 years), ideally within financial services or a data\-rich environment; we will also consider recent graduates with relevant skills or internships * Experience with reference data, data management, and data analytics * Working knowledge of tools and techniques for analysing and presenting data, such as SQL, Python/R, and Power BI (or similar tools) * Familiarity with data vendor products (e.g., Bloomberg, Reuters) is beneficial * Interest in or exposure to advanced analytics, anomaly detection, or AI\-driven data solutions is an advantage **The Purpose of Your Role** In this role, you will balance day\-to\-day data quality and production activities with analytical and project\-based work, with a strong focus on quality and continuous improvement. You will also have the opportunity to contribute to the evolution of our data capabilities, including the use of automation, anomaly detection, and AI techniques to enhance data quality, efficiency, and insight generation. **The Skills You Bring** * A proactive and curious mindset, with the ability to work both independently and collaboratively * Strong investigative and analytical skills, with the ability to quickly understand complex issues and identify patterns or anomalies in data * An interest in innovation and continuous process improvement * Clear written and verbal communication skills, particularly when explaining complex topics * Strong interpersonal skills and the ability to build effective relationships with business partners * The ability to manage priorities and deliver against both short\-term deadlines and longer\-term objectives **The Value You Deliver** * Deliver high\-quality, accurate, and timely data analysis and insights * Identify and implement process improvements to enhance efficiency and data quality * Provide root cause analysis and insights into data challenges, including identifying anomalies and trends * Collaborate with business and technology partners to resolve issues and improve processes * Support the adoption of automation and AI\-driven approaches to strengthen data management * Proactively contribute to and support a range of projects and initiatives

title: Digital & Social Media Executive (B2C Retail Content)
description: **Location:** Citywest, Dublin (Hybrid options available) **Package:** €38k – €40k \+ 10% Bonus \+ Pension \+ Life Assurance **Requirements:** B2C Content, ideally in Retail, \& Full Drivers Licence needed **The Opportunity: Drive the Digital Voice of a National Irish Brand** Are you a creative marketer who loves to get out from behind the desk? We are representing a well\-established **Irish retail success story** with a nationwide network of over 100\+ stores. They are looking for a **Digital \& Social Media Executive** to be the creative digital engine for their brand. This is a content\-first role where you will travel to locations across the country, capturing the authentic stories, expertise, and community connections that make this group a household name in the **retail sector.** **The Role** Reporting to the Marketing Manager, you will lead the planning, execution, and optimisation of digital activity across a major national brand. * **Content Creation:** Produce engaging photography, graphics, and short\-form video (Reels/TikToks) that showcase products and store activity. * **Social Media Management:** Own the end\-to\-end content calendar, scheduling, and community engagement across all brand channels. * **On\-the\-Road Storytelling:** Travel nationwide to capture “real\-life” content—from staff expertise to local community projects and initiatives. * **Campaign Impact:** Support national retail promotions and seasonal trading through email marketing and paid social activity. * **Collaboration:** Work with local store owners and external partners to ensure all digital activity aligns with the brand’s identity and mission. **Requirements** * **Road Ready:** A **Full Irish Driving Licence** is essential as the role involves travel to stores nationwide. * **Experience:** 2–3 years in a Digital Marketing or Social Media role. * **Sector Savvy:** Experience in a **Retail or Trade environment** is a distinct advantage. * **Content Skills:** A natural eye for engaging visuals and strong copywriting skills. Experience with video editing tools is a plus. * **Education:** Third\-level qualification in Marketing or a related discipline. **Why Join?** * **Competitive Package:** €38k–€40k DOE, plus a performance bonus of up to 10%. * **Future\-Proofing:** 8% Company Pension contribution and Life Assurance. * **Work\-Life Balance:** Incremental annual leave plus two additional company days. * **Flexibility:** Initially site\-based with h **ybrid working options** available following successful probation.

title: ECO Support Administrator
description: **Base Location:** You'll be expected to spend 50% of your working week in our Dublin office **Salary:** €32,600 \- €41,500 \+ performance\-related bonus \+ a range of benefits to support your finances, wellbeing and family. **Working Pattern:** 12\-month Fixed Term Contract \| Full Time \| Flexible First options available **The role** Our ECO (Energy Company Obligations) Support Administrator will help fulfil SSE Airtricity’s ECO Obligations and SEAI grant scheme requirements and will help support Ireland’s transition to a low carbon economy. **You will** * Schedule bookings with customers and deal with queries from multiple channels. * Manage our Quality Management System manual and processes. * Ensure we meet all the standards, T\&Cs and requirements of the SEAI grant programmes. * Manage all our ECO credits which are to be loaded on to the SEAI system, with an annual value of more than €2m. * Provide associated administrative support and reporting. **You have** * Strong Microsoft Office skills (Word, Access, Excel, Outlook), with demonstrable experience working with Pivot Tables and LOOKUP's. * A background in customer care, assurance or compliance. * Experience in all aspects of office administration with excellent communication and call handling skills. * Knowledge of SEAI grant management and the energy efficiency industry. * The ability to build and maintain strong working relationships with suppliers, contractors and external stakeholders. **About SSE** SSE’s purpose is to provide energy needed today while building a better world of energy for tomorrow. title: Ecologist
company: Collins McNicholas Recruitment & HR Services Group
description: **Job Title:** Ecologist **Location:** Dublin \- position can be worked on a remote basis with just 1 day week travel to Dublin.The role is primarily desk\-based during the winter period, with site visits increasing to approximately one to max two days per week in the summer, depending on survey schedules. We have an excellent opportunity for an Ecologist to join our partner, a leading environmental consultancy working across ecology, land management, and data science to support improved biodiversity outcomes. They specialise in ecological assessment and enhancement for public and private sites across a range of land uses. Their services include ecological baseline assessments, biodiversity management planning, monitoring, and the development of technology\-led tools to support ecological work. The ideal candidate for this role would be someone considering going freelance or someone who wants to work on biodiversity enhancement projects. This position provides significant career growth potential and the opportunity to actively contribute to the future development of the company. **Key Responsibilities** * Lead and deliver ecological projects from scoping through to final reporting. * Design, undertake, and supervise habitat and species surveys in line with best practice and current guidance. * Produce high\-quality ecological reports, including baseline assessments, habitat evaluations, and biodiversity enhancement plans. * Interpret ecological data to support evidence\-led decision\-making and land management. * Liaise directly with clients and project partners in a clear and professional manner. * Provide expert ecological input and quality assurance across a range of projects. * Contribute to the ongoing development of methods, templates, and internal standards. * Mentor and support junior team members as the team grows (where relevant). **Key Requirements** * Degree (or higher) in Ecology or a related discipline. * Minimum of 7 years? professional experience working as a practising ecologist in Ireland. * Full membership (or eligibility) of a relevant professional body (e.g., **CIEEM** ). * Strong field skills, particularly in habitat survey and assessment. * Competence in Irish and EU ecological policy, guidance, and wildlife legislation. * Ability to work independently and manage multiple workstreams and projects. * Excellent written English with experience producing clear, well\-structured technical reports. * Working knowledge of GIS mapping systems (QGIS / QField). * Full clean driving licence and willingness to undertake fieldwork. **For a confidential discussion and more information on this position please contact Gillian Nicholson** gillian.nicholson@collinsmcnicholas.ie 090 645 0761

title: English (Ireland) Language Specialist - Freelance AI Trainer Project
description: Are you an English language expert eager to shape the future of AI? Large‑scale language models are evolving from clever chatbots into powerful engines of linguistic discovery. With high‑quality training data, tomorrow's AI can democratize world‑class education, keep pace with cutting‑edge research, and streamline communication for English speakers everywhere. That training data begins with you—we need your expertise to help power the next generation of AI. We're looking for English language specialists who live and breathe the intricacies of English grammar, syntax, morphology, semantics, phonetics, and pragmatics. You'll challenge advanced language models on topics like sentence structure, verb tense and aspect, word usage, idiomatic expressions, pronunciation, and cultural context—documenting every failure mode so we can harden model reasoning. On a typical day, you will converse with the model on language scenarios, verify factual accuracy and logical soundness, capture reproducible error traces, and suggest improvements to our prompt engineering and evaluation metrics. A degree in English language, linguistics, or a closely related field is ideal; peer‑reviewed publications, teaching experience, or hands\-on linguistic analysis projects signal fit. Clear, metacognitive communication—"showing your work"—is essential. Ready to turn your English language expertise into the knowledge base for tomorrow's AI? Apply today and start teaching the model that will teach the world. We offer a pay range of $6\-to\- $65 per hour, with the exact rate determined after evaluating your experience, expertise, and geographic location. Final offer amounts may vary from the pay range listed above. As a contractor you'll supply a secure computer and high‑speed internet; company‑sponsored benefits such as health insurance and PTO do not apply. **Employment type:** Contract **Workplace type:** Remote **Seniority level:** Entry Level

title: Entrepreneurship Instructor (AI Training)
description: **About The Role** We're looking for experienced entrepreneurship educators and business experts to help evaluate and improve AI systems trained on startups, innovation, and business planning. Your real\-world knowledge will directly shape how AI understands and communicates the principles of building and scaling businesses — making a meaningful impact on the next generation of entrepreneurship tools. * Organization: Alignerr (Powered by Labelbox) * Type: Hourly / Task\-Based Contract * Location: Remote * Commitment: 10–40 hours/week **What You'll Do** * Review AI\-generated startup scenarios, business plans, and entrepreneurship content for accuracy and practical relevance * Assess the quality of business models, financial assumptions, and go\-to\-market strategies * Identify unrealistic projections, flawed reasoning, or gaps in early\-stage decision\-making logic * Provide structured, actionable feedback to help AI produce more reliable and grounded business guidance * Work independently and asynchronously on your own schedule **Who You Are** * 3\+ years of experience teaching entrepreneurship, small business, or startup strategy * Strong understanding of business models, venture stages, and early\-stage decision\-making * Able to critically evaluate written business plans and identify what's missing or misleading * Comfortable delivering clear, applied, real\-world feedback at scale * Self\-motivated and reliable in an asynchronous remote environment **Nice to Have** * MBA or entrepreneurship\-focused graduate degree * Experience as a founder, startup advisor, or angel investor * Familiarity with AI tools, content evaluation workflows, or edtech platforms **Why Join Us** * Work on cutting\-edge AI projects with top research labs * Fully remote and flexible — work on your own schedule * Freelance perks: autonomy, variety, and global collaboration * Contribute to meaningful work that improves how AI understands business and entrepreneurship * Potential for ongoing work and contract extension

title: Environmental Advisor
description: We are currently seeking experienced and talented Advisors to join our high performing project team. If you would like to join a company who wins high profile projects with blue chip clients and if you enjoy working in a stimulating environment, then Flynn is the company for you. REPORTING TO: ESG Lead Purpose of Role This will involve the day\-to\-day management of all environmental related activities compliance along with a series of environmental and sustainability\-based projects. Role Responsibilities * Conduct environmental inspections and audits across project sites to monitor compliance with environmental legislation, company procedures, and project\-specific requirements. * Provide practical environmental advice and support to project teams, assisting in the implementation of environmental controls and best practices. * Compile, review, and update Environmental Management Plans (EMPs) and Waste Management Plans (WMPs) in line with project requirements and regulatory obligations. * Monitor environmental performance on projects, identifying risks, non\-conformances, and opportunities for continual improvement. * Support site teams in achieving compliance with planning conditions, licences, permits, and client environmental requirements. * Undertake inspections relating to waste segregation, pollution prevention, water management, spill response, dust, noise, and biodiversity protection measures. * Assist in incident investigations, including environmental near misses, spills, and non\-conformances, ensuring corrective actions are implemented. * Deliver environmental awareness and toolbox talks / Workshops to site personnel and contractors to promote good environmental practices. * Liaise with project stakeholders, subcontractors, regulators, and clients on environmental matters as required. * Maintain accurate environmental records, audit findings, inspection reports, waste documentation, and compliance trackers. * Support sustainability and waste reduction initiatives, promoting resource efficiency and environmental best practices across projects. * Assist in the preparation of reports, KPIs, and environmental performance data for internal and external reporting. * Ensuring compliance to regulatory licencing requirements. * Directing site activities towards achieving energy, carbon, water, and waste reduction targets, through measuring, analysis, and reporting programmes. * Capture, analysis and reporting of all relevant data relating to effluent, energy, water, waste, and sustainability. * Work with cross\-functional teams to maintain environmental management and monitoring programmes; * Assist with the maintenance of ISO14001 Environmental Management System on site; * Complete site energy/environmental audits to identify opportunities for improvement and improve sites resilience to climate change. * Develop learning opportunities and training plans for staff and manage delivery of environmental weeks on site. * Liaise with the group environmental team on upcoming sustainability projects and strategies. * Develop and maintain company Environmental \& Sustainability standards, policies, systems and programs applicable to our projects * Drive and lead site Environmental \& Sustainability functions to align with corporate goals and develop programs and continuous improvement efforts * Provide Enviromental leadership and direction for management, professional, technical and support staff across the site * Ensure systems are in place to enable site to operate in legal and ethical compliance with regard to EHS issues * Promote employee awareness / training on matters of environment, health and safety * Other duties as assigned by senior management The Candidate * Third level qualification in EHS or a related discipline. * 3\+ years’ experience working on large scale commercial/construction projects. * Highly organised and capable of managing multiple projects simultaneously. * Conscientious, highly focused and detail orientated. * Self\-starter and the ability to build relationships with all stakeholders. * Full clean driver’s license preferable. * Excellent communication skills. Remuneration Flynn offer a competitive remuneration package to include basic salary, pension and life assurance, healthcare, and a car allowance/company vehicle.

title: Field Service Engineer 3
description: Requisition ID 197682 Date posted 06/02/2026 Work Location Model On\-site Work Location Dublin\-IE\-Remote Work Country Ireland**The group you’ll be a part of** --------------------------------- The Customer Support Business Group focuses on enabling our customers with premier customer support throughout their lifecycle with Lam. We drive performance, productivity, safety, and quality of customers installed base performance and deliver service and lifecycle solutions for their most critical equipment and processes. **The impact you’ll make** -------------------------- As a Field Service Engineer at Lam, you will step into the limelight of customer support. Your work goes beyond installations and troubleshooting; it ensures the operational excellence of our complex semiconductor equipment. You're on the front lines, understanding customer needs and collaborating with various teams to deliver solutions. This is a full time position, based in Kildare, Ireland. **What you’ll do** ------------------ * Provide quality on\-site repair, maintenance service, and complete installation/relocation start\-up services of Lam’s complex electro\-mechanical and electronic systems at customer sites. * Perform analytical problem\-solving, troubleshooting, conduct diagnostics, and isolate issues to the component level, and take corrective actions to minimize downtime, system interruptions, and equipment malfunctions. * Keep up to date on semiconductor capital equipment and Lam products through learning, training, and certification. * Assist in complex field modifications and updates, maintaining records and reports to coordinate activities between Lam and the customer. * Serve as a subject matter expert, representing Lam to our valued customers, delivering exceptional customer satisfaction. * Troubleshoot and create customer response action plans to support customer needs. * Transparently discuss opportunities and challenges with customer to increase trust. * Provide technical instruction and mentoring for more junior engineers. **Who we’re looking for** ------------------------- **Minimum Qualifications:** * Bachelor’s degree in Electronics, Engineering, or related field and 5\+ years of experience; or Master’s degree with 3\+ years; or equivalent work experience. * Previous experience in installation, maintenance, repair, or troubleshooting of semiconductor capital equipment. * Proficiency in interpreting electrical and mechanical schematics, diagrams, and operation manuals. * Able to work in a clean room environment while wearing personal protective safety equipment, or cleanroom suit. * Frequently position oneself (e.g., bend, stoop, reach, walk, sit, twist, squat, kneel, climb, stand) to perform job tasks for extended periods. * Able to travel frequently, domestically and internationally, based on customer needs, flexibility to work a variety shifts including compressed and alternative workweeks including overtime and on\-call if needed. **Preferred qualifications** ---------------------------- * Proficiency in basic computer skills and familiarity with common office software including Microsoft Office Suite. * Communication skills, both written and verbal, in English. * Foundational analytical and problem\-solving skills. **Our commitment** ------------------ We believe it is important for every person to feel valued, included, and empowered to achieve their full potential. By bringing unique individuals and viewpoints together, we achieve extraordinary results. Lam Research ("Lam" or the "Company") is an equal opportunity employer. Lam is committed to and reaffirms support of equal opportunity in employment and non\-discrimination in employment policies, practices and procedures on the basis of race, religious creed, color, national origin, ancestry, physical disability, mental disability, medical condition, genetic information, marital status, sex (including pregnancy, childbirth and related medical conditions), gender, gender identity, gender expression, age, sexual orientation, or military and veteran status or any other category protected by applicable federal, state, or local laws. It is the Company's intention to comply with all applicable laws and regulations. Company policy prohibits unlawful discrimination against applicants or employees. *Lam offers a variety of work location models based on the needs of each role. Our hybrid roles combine the benefits of on\-site collaboration with colleagues and the flexibility to work remotely and fall into two categories – On\-site Flex and Virtual Flex. ‘On\-site Flex’ you’ll work 3\+ days per week on\-site at a Lam or customer/supplier location, with the opportunity to work remotely for the balance of the week. ‘Virtual Flex’ you’ll work 1\-2 days per week on\-site at a Lam or customer/supplier location, and remotely the rest of the time.*

title: Finance Supervisor

title: Financial Advisor
description: We are currently working with a Wealth Management firm who are looking to hire a Financial Advisor to join their team in Dublin. This position involves managing an established client portfolio while also focusing on growing new business opportunities. Responsibilities of this **Financial Advisor** Job: * Manage and grow an existing portfolio of clients, ensuring a proactive client focused service. * Drive new business growth through referrals, self\-generated leads, and existing client relationships. * Ensure all client records are maintained accurately. * Conduct thorough research and analysis to recommend financial solutions that address a wide range of client needs. * Stay ahead of industry trends, regulatory changes and market developments Requirements of this **Financial Advisor** Job: * Fully QFA qualified * In\-depth knowledge of financial regulations, markets and industry practices * Demonstrable experience in financial advisory, with a proven track record of managing and growing client portfolios * Excellent communication and interpersonal skills * A high level of commercial awareness For more information on this **Financial Advisor** Job or to discuss in more detail, contact Aoife Stokes at astokes@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly below.

title: Financial Analyst
description: A leading global law firm based in Dublin 2 is currently seeking to hire a Financial Analyst to join its high\-performing Finance team. Reporting directly to the Senior Finance Manager, this is an excellent opportunity for a newly or recently qualified ACA / ACCA accountant who is keen to develop their career within a dynamic, collaborative, and technology\-driven environment. This role offers strong exposure across the business and would particularly suit someone with an interest in financial analysis, reporting, systems, and process improvement. **Key Responsibilities** * Drive and maintain monthly reporting and KPI analysis across departments and individual fee earners * Deliver detailed financial analysis to support strategic decision\-making * Collaborate with the Finance Management team on reporting and presentations * Liaise with Partners and internal stakeholders on finance\-related queries * Support and mentor trainees where required * Contribute to finance projects, process improvements, and system enhancements * Assist with ad hoc reporting and analysis * Present KPI performance insights at fee earner level * Act as a key liaison between Finance and IT regarding the practice management system * Support software implementations and continuous improvement initiatives **Candidate Profile** * ACA / ACCA qualified with a strong academic background * 1–2 years’ PQE preferred, though newly qualified candidates will also be considered * Strong analytical and problem\-solving abilities * Excellent attention to detail and reconciliation skills * Advanced IT and Excel skills * Experience with finance systems or implementations is advantageous * Strong communication and interpersonal skills * Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment * Proactive, organised, and collaborative approach **The Opportunity** * Excellent salary and benefits package * Hybrid working model * Genuine opportunities for career progression and internal development * Exposure to a highly regarded international professional services environment To apply or discuss this opportunity confidentially, please contact Richard Minchin at Lex Consultancy.

title: Financial Analyst
description: **We are seeking a Financial Analyst to join a growing organisation operating within the energy and infrastructure sector, to support a portfolio of large\-scale projects. The business is operating in a capital\-intensive environment and continues to expand, offering strong exposure across project delivery, commercial decision\-making, and senior stakeholder engagement.** **Key Responsibilities:** * Partner with project and senior stakeholders to provide financial insight, challenge assumptions, and support decision\-making. * Support budgeting, forecasting, and business case development, including scenario analysis and cost evaluation. * Prepare and review monthly management accounts, including variance analysis and clear performance commentary. * Maintain strong financial controls and support audit, compliance, and governance requirements across projects. * Contribute to financial planning, cashflow forecasting, and the ongoing development of finance processes. **Key Requirements:** * Qualified accountant (ACA / ACCA / CIMA or equivalent) with approximately 1 years’ post\-qualification experience. * Experience in a finance business partnering or commercially focused finance role. * Background in a capital\-intensive environment such as energy, infrastructure, construction, or property. * Strong experience in management accounting, budgeting, forecasting, and financial analysis. * Confident communicator with the ability to engage and challenge non\-finance stakeholders. **This role offers the opportunity to develop business partnering experience within a commercial finance environment, supporting key projects within a Joint Venture alongside major industry organisations.** For a confidential discussion, please contact Matthew Fleming at mfleming@elevatepartners.ie.

title: Financial Modeller - Renewable Energy
description: **On behalf of our client, a leading renewable energy firm, we are seeking an experienced and detail\-oriented Financial Modeller to join their high\-performing team.** *The successful candidate will be responsible for developing and maintaining sophisticated financial models to support investment decisions, acquisitions, and strategic planning across the business.* **Responsibilities of the Financial Modeller role:** * Develop and deliver robust and transparent project finance models for investment appraisal, acquisition analysis, forecasting, and debt structuring. * Mentor and support junior team members in best practices for financial modelling and model governance. * Ensure data integrity by applying stringent validation processes and incorporating robust checks and controls within all models. * Review and improve existing models, including those prepared externally, ensuring they align with business needs and financial modelling standards. **Requirements of the Financial Modeller role:** * A minimum of 3 years’ post\-qualification experience, with a strong academic background in a numerate discipline (minimum 2:1 degree). * Proven expertise in project finance modelling, with advanced Excel skills and working knowledge of VBA. * Strong communication skills, with experience in preparing reports and presentations for senior stakeholders. * Ability to work collaboratively on multi\-disciplinary teams, demonstrating strong analytical and business diagnostic skills. **For more information or to discuss this opportunity in more detail, contact cogrady@elevatepartners.ie or apply directly via the link below.**

title: Fleet Operations Supervisor
description: **Essential:** * Driving Licence : Full: B * Minimum Experience Required (Years) : **2 Years of vehicle maintenance supervisor Experience** * Minimum Qualification : **Level 7 (incl Diploma \& Ordinary Bachelor Degree)** **Desirable:** * Ability Skills : **Computer Literacy, Engineering** * Competency Skills : **Decision Making, Initiative, Management, Time Management** * Additional Skills : **ForkLift of Counter Balance** * Specialisation In : **N/A** The Fleet Operations Supervisor shall be responsible for the operational oversight, coordination, maintenance scheduling, compliance management and logistical supervision of the Company’s commercial vehicle fleet, trailers, plant machinery and transport\-related assets. The role involves ensuring the safe, efficient and compliant operation of fleet activities in support of the Company’s nationwide event logistics and transport operations. Key Responsibilities Coordinate and supervise the daily operation, deployment and scheduling of the Company fleet and transport assets; Oversee preventative maintenance programmes for commercial vehicles, trailers, forklifts and plant equipment; Liaise with external maintenance contractors, testing centres and service providers to ensure fleet reliability and roadworthiness; Maintain fleet service records, inspection documentation, maintenance schedules and compliance reports; Monitor compliance with Road Safety Authority (RSA), transport safety and workplace health \& safety requirements; Coordinate CVRT inspections, servicing schedules, defect reporting and vehicle downtime management; Supervise fleet asset tracking, fuel usage monitoring and operational efficiency reporting; Support transport logistics planning for nationwide event operations and commercial deliveries; Assist with inventory control and fleet\-related equipment management; Conduct routine operational inspections and compliance checks across transport and fleet assets; Coordinate vehicle allocation and operational readiness to meet project and event schedules; Assist management with fleet budgeting, supplier coordination and operational planning; Support implementation of fleet management systems, operational procedures and compliance protocols; and Ensure that all fleet operations are conducted in accordance with company policies and Irish transport regulations. Fleet \& Equipment Under Supervision The Company fleet and operational equipment currently includes commercial vans, rigid trucks, articulated transport vehicles, trailers, forklifts, access equipment, utility vehicles and specialist transport assets used in nationwide event and logistics operations. Pay: €42,910\.40 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Full Stack Developer
description: **About RWS** RWS is a global AI solutions company empowering the world’s most trusted enterprise AI. Our proprietary Cultural Intelligence Layer, powered by 250,000 data specialists, cultural and language experts and deep domain professionals, backed by 45\+ patents, makes enterprise AI culturally fluent, contextually accurate and secure, ensuring every interaction reflects a brand’s tone, context and customer values. Through our Generate, Transform and Protect segments, we deliver intelligent content, enterprise knowledge, large\-scale localization and IP protection for global growth. Trusted by 80\+ of the world’s top 100 brands, RWS provides the confidence, governance and expertise organizations need to deploy AI safely, responsibly and at scale. Headquartered in the UK, RWS is listed on AIM (RWS.L). More information: rws.com. **About Propylon** Propylon is the Dublin\-based content‑management arm within the RWS group, specialising in software for legal and regulatory workflows. For more than 20 years, Propylon has helped governments, standard bodies and regulated organisations manage legislation, regulations and legal content in a smarter, modular way. Joining Propylon means working in a people\-led, innovation\-driven environment recognised as one of Ireland’s Best Workplaces in Tech \- while having the backbone and global reach of RWS. You’d be contributing to meaningful projects that shape law, compliance and global content workflows, with the support and flexibility to grow your skills and career. **Job Purpose** We have an exciting opportunity for a Full Stack Developer to join our team in expanding our core product offering and working across major client projects. The candidate will be an experienced engineer with several years of experience in a similar role and will have worked with modern frontend and backend applications in a professional context. **Job Overview** **Responsibilities:** * Work with technical team leads to design overall application architecture * Drive development across backend and frontend components * Contribute to product architecture and design * Participate in code reviews **Requirements:** * Excellent understanding of the Python programming language, its ecosystem, and idioms. * Significant professional experience with Python based MVC framework, preferably Django. * Excellent working knowledge of SQL based RDBMS, e.g. Postgres, MySQL. * Experience with modern Python features such as type hinting, async, data classes, etc. * Experience developing applications and clients that utilize data and functionality exposed via REST, GraphQL, RPC etc. * Familiarity with Docker and related containerization technologies. **Nice to haves:** * Working knowledge of a modern JavaScript framework, preferably React * Experience with XML processing/transformation. * Experience with document management/document workflow software. * Familiarity or work experience with DevOps workflow/principles, building CI/CD pipelines. **Personal Attributes:** * Wants to be part of a team that is committed to delivering high quality innovative products. * Wants to be part of a culture that is committed to sharing and enhancing the knowledge of the wider team, supporting team wide learning and success * Have a curious mindset – about what you do, the technology you use and how you connect with others to deliver products that we are proud to deliver to our clients. **Life at RWS** **Life at RWS** **\-** If you like the idea of working with smart people who are passionate about growing the value of ideas, data and content by making sure organizations are understood , then you’ll love life at RWS. Our purpose is to unlock global understanding. This means our work fundamentally recognizes the value of every language and culture. So, we celebrate difference, we are inclusive and believe that diversity makes us strong. We want every employee to grow as an individual and excel in their career. In return, we expect all our people to live by the values that unite us: **to partner** , putting clients fist and winning together **, to pioneer** , innovating fearlessly and leading with vision and courage, **to progress** , aiming high and growing through actions and **to deliver** , owning the outcome and building trust with our colleagues and clients. **RWS embraces DEI** and promotes equal opportunity, we are an Equal Opportunity Employer and prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind. RWS is committed to the principle of equal employment opportunity for all employees and to providing employees with a work environment free of discrimination and harassment. All employment decisions at RWS are based on business needs, job requirements and individual qualifications, without regard to race, religion, nationality, ethnicity, sex, age, disability, or sexual orientation. RWS will not tolerate discrimination based on any of these characteristics. **RWS Values** Get the 3Ps right – Partner, Pioneer, Progress – and we´ll Deliver together as RWS. **Recruitment Agencies:** RWS Holdings PLC does not accept agency resumes. Please do not forward any unsolicited resumes to any RWS employees. Any unsolicited resume received will be treated as the property of RWS and Terms \& Conditions associated with the use of such resume will be considered null and void.

description: **Full Stack Developer (Python / React / AWS)** Salary: €60,000 to €85,000 DOE \+ bonus \+ benefits \+ Hybrid: 3 days in the office \- Dublin City Centre Our client is a global financial services company that provides technology and operational solutions to investment funds, multinational companies and institutional investors worldwide. title: General Assistant
description: As a **General Assistant** , you will play a vital front‑of‑house role within a fast‑paced, client‑facing environment. This is an in‑person position supporting workplace services across four floors, where you will be a visible, proactive, and highly reliable presence for our client teams. This is a **full time, 37\.5 hours per week, working Monday to Friday.** You will be the “go‑to” person day‑to‑day — ensuring spaces are well\-presented, stocked, and operating smoothly, and that excellent communication and service standards are consistently delivered. No previous experience is required, as full training will be provided. **Key Responsibilities** * Front‑of‑house duties, providing a warm, professional, and confident presence * Acting as a key point of contact for client queries and requests * Stocking and maintaining pantry and refreshment areas across four floors * Supporting workplace services, including moving furniture, desks, and equipment as required * Assisting with event setup, room layouts, and general preparation * Basic food preparation, counter setup, and serving of food and beverages when needed * Ensuring the highest level of customer care is delivered to clients and visitors * Following all food hygiene and Health \& Safety guidelines (training provided) * Ensuring correct storage and handling of food items and equipment * Supporting the on‑site Supervisor and colleagues in a flexible, positive manner * Adapting to a fast\-moving workload with a proactive, problem\-solving mindset **About You** * Strong, clear communication skills * A friendly, professional, front‑of‑house approach * Versatile, adaptable, and comfortable working across multiple floors * A proactive “can\-do” attitude * Good teamwork and interpersonal skills * Willingness to learn and support a variety of tasks * A good level of English * Previous catering, hospitality, or customer service experience is beneficial but not essential At Compass Ireland, our people are our business. We will not retain any CV's or job applications for longer than necessary. title: Geotechnical/Geoenvironmental Associate
description: The Team: Cundall’s Geotechnical and Geoenvironmental Engineering team pride ourselves on our exemplary track record of providing innovative and sustainable ground engineering solutions, focussed on ensuring the best possible construction design advice that improves and protects the environment in which we live, while providing the facilities for day\-to\-day life. The Geotechnical Engineering team are an award\-winning source of expertise – and offer a pro\-active and collaborative approach to skill development, and professional excellence. The Role: This is an excellent opportunity for an experienced Geoenvironmental Engineer, Geotechnical Engineer or an Engineering Geologist with an environmental focus, to join our growing Dublin office. You will get the opportunity to work on the design of some of the most exciting construction projects both locally and worldwide. Working closely with colleagues across our multi\-disciplinary practice (in particular, our Civil and Structural Engineering teams), you will provide specialist consultancy input on projects across all sectors including Education, Healthcare, Critical Systems, Industrial, Residential and Retail. In this wide\-ranging role, you will undertake detailed ground engineering analysis, lead site investigations and risk assessments (appropriate to your skill set) as well as develop ground engineering design solutions for complex challenges. You will be required to manage and mentor junior staff and undertake detailed technical reviews of reports. The Skills: Ideally holding chartership and with significant demonstrable relevant experience (e.g. a minimum of 8 years in consultancy), along with a BSc degree (or equivalent) in geology, engineering geology, earth sciences, civil engineering, geotechnical engineering, geochemistry or environmental science, you have extensive experience in a broad range of ground engineering work appropriate to your skill set. An MSc is preferable. You will have extensive experience of scoping, conducting and interpreting site investigations for a wide variety of schemes including for large / tall buildings in complex urban environments, and for masterplanning. Ideally you will be able to demonstrate some experience within the data centre and energy sectors as well as having temporary works experience. To succeed in this role, you will be able to show a broad understanding of engineering geology as well as being a specialist in your skill set. You will have proven working knowledge guidance including BS 5930/Eurocode 7, BS10175, CIRIA 552, 665, 733 \& 758, BS8485, BRE\-SD1, LCRM and WM3\. You will demonstrate the ability to us ground engineering software relevant to your area of expertise. You will be able to demonstrate your ability to manage and develop more junior members of the team and relate to more senior ground engineering staff and other Cundall engineering disciplines in a supportive and collaborative manner. You will have proven experience in building client relationships both externally and internally. Given the collaborative and client focussed nature of this role (both internal and external), excellent communication skills and experience of using your initiative and negotiating skills to overcome challenges are essential. Job Description Your career at Cundall **We know that to be the best at what we do we need a diverse workforce underpinned by an inclusive culture. Creating environments where everyone can find joy doing their best work is also great for everyone. That’s why we positively welcome applications from people of all backgrounds and identities, and encourage, support, and celebrate the diverse voices of our people.****\#LI\-JP1**

title: Graduate Building Surveyor
description: JLL supports the Whole You, personally and professionally. Our people at JLL are shaping the future of real estate for a better world by combining world class services, advisory and technology to our clients. We are committed to hiring the best, most talented people in our industry; and we support them through professional growth, flexibility, and personalized benefits to manage life in and outside of work. Whether you’ve got deep experience in commercial real estate, skilled trades, and technology, or you’re looking to apply your relevant experience to a new industry, we empower you to shape a brighter way forward so you can thrive professionally and personally. About JLL We’re a professional services and investment management firm specializing in real estate. We help organizations around the world achieve their ambitions by owning, occupying and investing in real estate. We’re a Fortune 500 company. We work across 80 countries, in 280 different offices, with a team of over 77,000 individuals and we look after a property portfolio of over 4bn square feet, all on behalf of our clients. If you’re looking to step up your career, JLL is the perfect professional home. At JLL, you’ll have a chance to innovate with the world’s leading businesses, put that expertise into action on landmark projects, and work on game\-changing real estate initiatives. You’ll also make long\-lasting professional connections through sharing different perspectives, and you’ll be inspired by the best. We’re focused on opportunity and want to help you make the most of yours. Achieve your ambitions – join us at JLL! Graduate Building Surveyor We are seeking to recruit an additional graduate Building Surveyor to work on commercial property portfolios owned/to be acquired/disposed of by our clients. What JLL can offer A chance to work with industry leaders An excellent range of work across all core building surveying competencies including technical due diligence surveys, defect analysis, dilapidations, building refurbishment and construction monitoring. Good support from the Building Surveying team and the company as a whole Counsellor/supervisor roles and support provided for APC process Strong base salary and bonus scheme Excellent company benefits including private health insurance Qualifications and other attributes considered important to the position are: Level 8 Qualification in Building Surveying/Post Grad Route Effective communicator with strong interpersonal skills Can\-do attitude Excellent IT (Microsoft Office and Autodesk) and Organisational Skills Ability to work independently using own initiative A capability for achieving tight targets and meeting deadlines Organised task manager who can handle a number of regular deadlines across multiple instructions concurrently What you can expect from us You’ll join an entrepreneurial, inclusive culture. One where we succeed together – across the desk and around the globe. Where like\-minded people work naturally together to achieve great things. Our Total Rewards program reflects our commitment to helping you achieve your ambitions in career, recognition, wellbeing, benefits and pay. We’ll offer you a competitive salary and benefits package. Join us to develop your strengths and enjoy a fulfilling career full of varied experiences. Keep those ambitions in sights and imagine where JLL can take you... If this job description resonates with you, we encourage you to apply even if you don’t meet all of the requirements below. We’re interested in getting to know you and what you bring to the table! Personalized benefits that support personal well\-being and growth: JLL recognizes the impact that the workplace can have on your wellness, so we offer a supportive culture and comprehensive benefits package that prioritizes mental, physical and emotional health. About JLL – We’re JLL—a leading professional services and investment management firm specializing in real estate. We have operations in over 80 countries and a workforce of over 102,000 individuals around the world who help real estate owners, occupiers and investors achieve their business ambitions. As a global Fortune 500 company, we also have an inherent responsibility to drive sustainability and corporate social responsibility. That’s why we’re committed to our purpose to shape the future of real estate for a better world. We’re using the most advanced technology to create rewarding opportunities, amazing spaces and sustainable real estate solutions for our clients, our people, and our communities. Our core values of teamwork, ethics and excellence are also fundamental to everything we do and we’re honored to be recognized with awards for our success by organizations both globally and locally. Creating a diverse and inclusive culture where we all feel welcomed, valued and empowered to achieve our full potential is important to who we are today and where we’re headed in the future. And we know that unique backgrounds, experiences and perspectives help us think bigger, spark innovation and succeed together.

This role requires a drive to seek new business opportunities, typically involving 70\-80 calls per day. * Generate leads through a variety of channels, including cold outreach, inbound marketing, and networking events. * Qualifying leads to determine their potential fit for our services and ensure that they meet our target customer profile. * Research potential clients and their industries to better understand their needs and pain points. * Build relationships with prospects and educate them on our services, using your knowledge of R\&D tax credits and incentives. * Schedule and coordinate meetings for our sales team with qualified prospects. * Collaborate with our marketing team to optimise lead generation efforts and improve our outreach strategies. * Maintain accurate records of lead and prospect interactions in our CRM system, and ensure that all data is up\-to\-date. * Participate in regular training and professional development to improve your knowledge of our services, industry trends, and sales techniques. * Achieve and exceed weekly, monthly, and quarterly lead generation targets. \#LI\-DH1

title: Graduate Sales Executive

title: Graduate Site Engineers / Geospatial Surveyors 2026
description: **Position:** Graduate Site Engineers / Geospatial Suveyors 2026 **Location** : Dublin, Kildare, Cork, Limerick, Gavle, Copenhagen, Groningen, Charleroi. **Start date:** We operate on a rolling recruitment basis. Please advise your availability to start in your application form. You can discuss this further with your recruiter upon application. **Company Description** We are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high\-quality, customer\-focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Waste to Energy (BioEnergy), Specialist Lift, Off\-Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling), and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, Continuous Professional Development (CPD) leading to chartered status, performance incentives, and the ability to progress your career with a world\-class engineering contractor. **Job Description** As a Graduate Site Engineer / Geospatial Surveyor, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the accurate positioning of mechanical services and equipment using the latest survey technologies and software. You will work closely with a multidisciplinary team of engineers and industry experts to deliver cutting\-edge projects. This role provides opportunities for career progression and development, with full\-site training provided on best\-in\-class instruments and standard industry software. Key Responsibilities * Perform detailed field and office surveying work (Survey Control Network / Laser Scanning / Setting Out). * Prepare and analyse data for construction accuracy. * Utilise modern survey methods and survey devices such as Total Stations and Mobile Laser Scanning devices. * Work with Post processing software such as AutoCAD, Leica Infinity, Navvis Ivion, Register 360, Starnet, Navisworks, etc * Attend Daily/Weekly Meetings with Client and Contractors to discuss project progress and deliver reports. * Any other duties as required by the contractor **Graduate Development Programme** As part of our commitment to your growth and career development, you will be enrolled in our comprehensive 2\-year Graduate Development Programme. This programme includes experiential learning, mentoring, professional development courses, and in\-house training on key skills such as technical writing, presentation, and time management. You will benefit from peer to peer learning and receive support for obtaining professional certifications. This structured approach ensures you gain the necessary skills and experience to progress in your career. **Skills And Requirements** To excel in this role, you should possess the following skills and qualifications: * Degree Qualification in Civil Engineering or Construction Management * Experience: Prior experience would be advantageous but is not essential * Qualifications: Additional qualifications would be advantageous but are not essential * Technological Proficiency: Knowledge of the latest survey technologies and software * Soft Skills: Strong team player with excellent interpersonal skills, organisational, and planning skills * Learning Mindset: Demonstrated appetite for continuous learning and personal development **Benefits** Be part of our Graduate Programme and contribute to meaningful projects that drive innovation and sustainability across multiple industries. We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles. Please note, we are currently not accepting applications from agencies, for this role at this time.

Manage business and self\-assessment tax filings for clients, ensuring timely completion. Contribute to the preparation of corporation tax returns, VAT returns, and management accounts.
