title: Graduate Validation Associate Scientist / Engineer (Manufacturing, Science & Technology)
description: **Working with Us** Challenging. Meaningful. Life\-changing. Those aren’t words that are usually associated with a job. But working at Bristol Myers Squibb is anything but usual. Here, uniquely interesting work happens every day, in every department. From optimizing a production line to the latest breakthroughs in cell therapy, this is work that transforms the lives of patients, and the careers of those who do it. You’ll get the chance to grow and thrive through opportunities uncommon in scale and scope, alongside high\-achieving teams. Take your career farther than you thought possible. Bristol Myers Squibb recognizes the importance of balance and flexibility in our work environment. We offer a wide variety of competitive benefits, services and programs that provide our employees with the resources to pursue their goals, both at work and in their personal lives. Read more: careers.bms.com/working\-with\-us. **Position Summary** We are seeking a Graduate Validation Associate Scientist / Engineer to join the Sterile Drug Product MS\&T team at BMS Cruiserath. This role is specifically designed for a recent graduate and provides a structured rotation across key manufacturing equipment and areas within the facility based **on a 2\-year fixed term contract.** You will work as part of a multidisciplinary team supporting sterile manufacturing operations, gaining broad exposure to equipment, processes, and validation activities across the site. With coaching from experienced engineers and scientists, you will build a strong technical foundation in pharmaceutical manufacturing and develop the skills needed for a long\-term career in MS\&T. Over time, the role will allow you to increase ownership and technical depth while supporting both project and sustaining activities within the facility. **What You Will Learn And Do** During your rotations, you will: * Gain hands\-on experience with different manufacturing equipment and process areas within a sterile drug product facility * Support cleaning validation, process validation, and equipment\-related activities under the guidance of senior team members * Develop and write validation protocols, Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), and validation reports. * Support risk assessments to identify critical parameters and acceptance criteria. * Execute validation studies, including cycle development, sampling, testing, and data analysis. * Assist in the preparation and execution of validation protocols, sampling activities, and technical reports * Learn how regulatory requirements (e.g. EU GMP, FDA) are applied in day\-to\-day manufacturing environments * Participate in cross\-functional teams with Manufacturing, Quality, Engineering and other functions * Support investigations, root cause analysis, and continuous improvement activities * Build knowledge of aseptic manufacturing, risk management and good documentation practices This role offers a strong learning pathway, with increasing responsibility as your capability and confidence grow. **Skills And Attributes We’re Looking For** * Curiosity and a strong desire to learn in a regulated manufacturing environment * Good problem\-solving and analytical skills * Clear written and verbal communication * Ability to work well in a team while developing independence over time * Attention to detail and appreciation for high\-quality documentation * Approaches challenges with a structured, logical, and data\-driven mindset * Takes ownership of assigned tasks and delivers to agreed timelines and quality standards * Willingness to learn and rotate across different equipment and technical areas **Qualifications \& Experience** * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Science, or a related discipline * Recent graduate or early\-career professional (industry experience not essential) * Interest in pharmaceutical or biotechnology manufacturing **Why you should apply** * You will help patients in their fight against serious diseases * You will be part of a company that encourages excellence and innovation, respects diversity, develops leaders and values its employees. * You’ll get a competitive salary and a great benefits package including an annual bonus, pension contribution, family medical allowance, 27 days of annual leave, life assurance and on\-site gym *If you come across a role that intrigues you but doesn’t perfectly line up with your resume, we encourage you to apply anyway. You could be one step away from work that will transform your life and career.* **Uniquely Interesting Work, Life\-changing Careers** With a single vision as inspiring as “Transforming patients’ lives through science™ ”, every BMS employee plays an integral role in work that goes far beyond ordinary. Each of us is empowered to apply our individual talents and unique perspectives in a supportive culture, promoting global participation in clinical trials, while our shared values of passion, innovation, urgency, accountability, inclusion and integrity bring out the highest potential of each of our colleagues. **On\-site Protocol** **Responsibilities** BMS has an occupancy structure that determines where an employee is required to conduct their work. This structure includes site\-essential, site\-by\-design, field\-based and remote\-by\-design jobs. The occupancy type that you are assigned is determined by the nature and responsibilities of your role: Site\-essential roles require 100% of shifts onsite at your assigned facility. Site\-by\-design roles may be eligible for a hybrid work model with at least 50% onsite at your assigned facility. For these roles, onsite presence is considered an essential job function and is critical to collaboration, innovation, productivity, and a positive Company culture. For field\-based and remote\-by\-design roles the ability to physically travel to visit customers, patients or business partners and to attend meetings on behalf of BMS as directed is an essential job function. **Supporting People With Disabilities** BMS is dedicated to ensuring that people with disabilities can excel through a transparent recruitment process, reasonable workplace accommodations/adjustments and ongoing support in their roles. Applicants can request a reasonable workplace accommodation/adjustment prior to accepting a job offer. If you require reasonable accommodations/adjustments in completing this application, or in any part of the recruitment process, direct your inquiries to adastaffingsupport@bms.com. Visit careers.bms.com/eeo\-accessibility to access our complete Equal Employment Opportunity statement. **Candidate Rights** BMS will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and conviction records, pursuant to applicable laws in your area. If you live in or expect to work from Los Angeles County if hired for this position, please visit this page for important additional information: https://careers.bms.com/california\-residents/ **Data Protection** We will never request payments, financial information, or social security numbers during our application or recruitment process. Learn more about protecting yourself at https://careers.bms.com/fraud\-protection. Any data processed in connection with role applications will be treated in accordance with applicable data privacy policies and regulations. If you believe that the job posting is missing information required by local law or incorrect in any way, please contact BMS at TAEnablement@bms.com. Please provide the Job Title and Requisition number so we can review. Communications related to your application should not be sent to this email and you will not receive a response. Inquiries related to the status of your application should be directed to Chat with Ripley. R1602520 : Graduate Validation Associate Scientist / Engineer (Manufacturing, Science \& Technology)

title: Guest Relations Porter (Valet Parking)
description: **Guest Relations Porter** You will receive training through our Dalata Academy to help you learn important skills. You will also meet experienced team members who can help you grow. At Dalata, we believe in promoting from within. If you work hard, you can have new opportunities in the future. Join us and be part of a friendly team! **Benefits** * Free employee meals on duty * 50% Off F\&B in all properties * €/£60 employee rate on rooms for all Dalata\-owned hotels * 30% off the Friends \& Family rate * Extra AL day for every year worked (5 days to a maximum of 5 days) * Employee Assistance Programme. * Cycle to Work Scheme/Transport Schemes * Pension access (Ask your HR Manager for local pension information) * Development Opportunities through our Dalata Academy\- support your career journey * Staff Appreciation Initiatives, fun team\-building activities and regular charity events **Your Job** * Monitor and maintain the car park and hotel grounds for the safety and comfort of our guests in a friendly and efficient manner. * Provide a professional \& competent security service for all car park users * Support the Vallet Parking of Guest cars * Maintain a high degree of health \& safety awareness * Be friendly, courteous and professional at all times. * Greet and welcome guests as they arrive at the hotel. * Register guests and process their payments. * Respond to any inquiries guests may have about in\-house facilities and local tourist information. * Ensure the security of room keys. * Update all guest information in the computer system. * Take and confirm reservations for guests **Requirements** * Fluent English and excellent communication skills. * Flexibility in working hours as the role includes mornings/evenings \& weekends * Ability to work as part of a team or on own initiative * Full Drivers Licence

title: HR Administrator
description: Key Outcomes * Support the HR Team with day\-to\-day administrative duties * HR mailbox management * Ensure data integrity in Workday and administer employee changes as appropriate * Meet deadlines to ensure critical people processes are met * Participate in wider HR team projects as required to ensure successful delivery and implementation * Assist and advise management \& staff on company policy queries in line with legislation. * Deal with day\-to\-day HR related queries from management and colleagues * Maintaining and updating the HR systems. * Attending meetings to provide general administrative support as required, taking and transcribing minutes as necessary. * Any other duties that may be required to support the HR team to deliver best practice HR services to employees and managers. Key Skills \& Experiences * HR qualification with 3\-4 years HR Experience desirable * Workday, TMS or Timepoint experience is desirable * Strong administration and organisational skills * Demonstrate a passion for HR and proven experience of adding value and driving change through progressive HR initiatives * Excellent Communication Skills with both internal and external parties * Attention to detail and the ability to multi\-task * Continuous Improvement – someone who is looking to constantly improve processes and ways of working. **Uniphar – Who we are** With a workforce of more than 3000 employees spread across Ireland, United Kingdom, Mainland Europe, MENA, and the USA, Uniphar is a trusted global partner to pharma and MedTech manufacturers, working to improve patient access to medicines around the world. Uniphar provides outsourced and specialized services to its clients, leveraging the strong relationships with 200\+ of the world’s best known pharmaco\-medical manufacturers across multiple geographies, enabled by our cutting\-edge digital technology and our highly expert teams. Uniphar is organised into three key divisions: Supply Chain Retail \& On Demand, Uniphar Med Tech and Uniphar Pharma. **Supply Chain \& Retail** Supply Chain Services provides both pre\-wholesale and wholesale distribution of pharmaceutical, healthcare and animal health products to pharmacies and hospital nationwide. The business supports a diverse customer base through the provision of strong service levels coupled with innovative commercial initiatives. In addition, the business provides services and supports that help independent community pharmacy to compete more effectively in an increasingly difficult environment and runs a network of Uniphar\-owned pharmacies under the Life, Allcare, McCauleys and Hickeys symbol brands. **Pharma – On Demand** The On\-Demand business is a specialist medical supply business that sources and distributes pharmaceuticals, medical equipment, and consumable supplies to healthcare professionals in over 160 countries worldwide. We have offices in Ireland, UK, Europe, USA and New Zealand. The company’s main divisions encompass everything from International Sales through Sales to NGO’s and Charities, Clinical Trials Supply, Imported (Unlicensed Medicines), Sexual Health Supplies, and the storage and distribution of medical products and devices for third parties**.** Click here to find out more: About Us \- Who we are \| Uniphar **Culture at Uniphar** Uniphar is a combination of many businesses each with its own identity, culture, values, and ways of working. Our overarching culture narrative is inclusive of each of these stores. Uniphar lives and breathes four key pillars which unpin and support our culture. We have a people first approach \- we do the right thing. We support entrepreneurial spirit where adaptability, commitment \& resilience is embodied in our way of working. We have a Common Purpose that connects our diversified businesses and people. Finally, Trust is at the heart of how we operate. Everything Uniphar does is enabled by our people and as we continue to grow domestically and internationally, we become more diverse. This diversity fuels our business and culture. **Sustainability** Sustainability is at the core of what we do and is deeply embedded in our business strategy. We want to contribute positively to the people and the world around us. Running our business in a sustainable way ensures that Uniphar will continue to prosper in the long\-term. We are focused on ensuring that each of the five pillars of our sustainability strategy are a fundamental part of our decision\-making process. **If you believe that you meet most of the criteria above, we encourage you to apply. We are an equal opportunities employer and will ensure any reasonable accommodations are implemented as part of the recruitment and selection process.** \#uniphargroup

description: **The Gowan Group**, a family\-owned holding Company controlling many successful trading Companies, is seeking to recruit a **Human Resources Administrator.** This role is fast\-paced, supporting our corporate business. To be successful in this role, you must enjoy working in a collaborative and changing environment, with the ability to multitask. The role is pivotal in providing a proactive, empathetic, professional, discreet and accurate HR service to our people through our administrative processes. Reporting to the HR Director, you will be responsible for the best\-in\-class delivery of our people operations services to the business. **Key Responsibilities:** * Support the recruitment process (posting adverts, scheduling interviews, shortlisting CVs, etc.). * Employee onboarding/inductions, including preparing contracts, new hire documentation, communications to management, finance and IT. * Liaise with payroll RE new starters/leavers/annual leave/contract changes etc. * Assist in supporting subsidiary internship programmes. * Superuser of the leave tracking system. * Arrange and title: Host
description: **Restaurant Host** Since 2006, Hawksmoor has been the UK’s home of ethically sourced, grass\-fed beef, cooked over charcoal and paired with award\-winning wines and cocktails. Hawksmoor Dublin plays a key role in bringing our warm, generous hospitality to the city. We’re proud to be a certified B Corp, one of the most sustainable restaurant groups in the industry, and to have been recognised as one of the Best Companies to work for year after year. As a **Restaurant Host** **at Hawksmoor** , you’ll be right at the heart of the guest experience. This is not a desk\-based role. You’ll be welcoming guests, managing reservations and waiting lists, coordinating seating and cloakroom, and supporting the floor team to keep service running smoothly. You’ll be a key part of how we deliver warm, generous hospitality from the very first hello. **What You’ll Do** * Welcome guests warmly and confidently on arrival * Manage reservations, walk\-ins and waiting lists during service * Seat guests and manage the flow of the restaurant to support smooth service * Oversee cloakroom and guest arrivals during busy periods * Communicate clearly with managers, floor teams and the kitchen * Support the floor team by helping set and reset tables and assist service when needed * Handle guest enquiries in person, by phone and email with care and professionalism **Who You Are** * Naturally friendly, confident and great with people * Comfortable working in a fast\-paced, busy restaurant environment * Organised, calm and able to think on your feet * A reliable team player who enjoys being part of a close\-knit team * Previous hospitality or customer\-facing experience is helpful but not essential, full training provided **Our Values** **Welcome Everyone** \- We create warm, inclusive spaces where people feel genuinely welcome. **Work Hard and Be Nice to People** \- We set high standards and treat each other with kindness and respect. **Hit Hawksmoor Standards** \- We care deeply about quality and consistency every day. **Keep Evolving** \- We stay curious, open to feedback and always looking to improve. **Benefits** * 30 days’ holiday * Tronc paid for every hour you work, including holidays and breaks * 25% Sunday Premium * Referral bonuses from day one * Paid annual volunteer days * A dedicated restaurant welfare budget for healthy snacks, drinks, and extra support during busy times * Free meals while you’re at work * 50% off food in our restaurants for you and your loved ones * HSF Health Plan – helping you look after your health and wellbeing * Access to Perkbox for exclusive discounts and perks * Taxsaver Commuter Ticket Scheme * Cycle to Work Scheme * Huge investment in training and development with qualifications across Wine, H\&S, Food Safety, HR and a five\-stage management development programme, run in partnership with the Watershed Management School We’re building a team that reflects the richness and diversity of the communities we serve. At Hawksmoor, we welcome everyone and believe in creating a workplace where individuality is celebrated, kindness is key and high standards are met together. If this role excites you but you don’t meet every requirement, please apply anyway. You might be exactly who we’re looking for.

title: IDC Facility Engineer
description: IDC Facility Engineer Country Ireland Project Type General Computing / Standard IDC Operations Level L1\-L2 / On\-site Facility Operations Support Function Critical Facility Systems, Electrical / Cooling / Environmental Operations Job Summary We are looking for an IDC Facility Engineer to support the daily operation and maintenance of data center facility systems in Finland and Ireland. The role focuses on stable operation of critical infrastructure, including electrical systems, power distribution, UPS, generators, cooling, ventilation, and environmental monitoring. This position is suitable for candidates with hands\-on experience in data center facilities, critical environments, electrical systems, HVAC, industrial maintenance, or building technical operations. The role starts from on\-site operational support and may grow into broader facility coordination or shift lead responsibilities depending on project needs and individual capability. Key Responsibilities * Support daily inspection, monitoring, and operation of data center facility systems, including power supply, UPS, generators, cooling, ventilation, and environmental control systems. * Follow standard operating procedures for routine checks, alarm response, escalation, and incident reporting. * Support preventive maintenance activities and coordinate with internal teams, vendors, and subcontractors when required. * Identify facility risks, abnormalities, or potential failures, and report them promptly according to escalation procedures. * Support first\-level troubleshooting for facility\-related issues under the guidance of senior engineers or technical leads. * Maintain accurate operation records, inspection logs, maintenance reports, and incident documentation. * Ensure all activities are carried out in compliance with site safety rules, customer requirements, and internal operational standards. * Cooperate with IT operations, asset management, security, and project delivery teams to ensure smooth daily data center operations. Requirements * Previous experience in data center facilities, critical facilities, electrical systems, HVAC, industrial maintenance, or building technical operations is preferred. * Basic understanding of electrical distribution, UPS, generators, cooling systems, or environmental monitoring systems. * Ability to follow SOPs, checklists, permit\-to\-work requirements, and safety procedures carefully. * Good sense of responsibility, safety awareness, and attention to detail. * Able to work in an on\-site data center environment and support shift work if required. * Good communication skills in English. Finnish or local language skills are an advantage depending on site needs. * Relevant technical education or certification in electrical, mechanical, HVAC, automation, facility management, or related fields is preferred. What We Value * Strong safety awareness, compliance mindset, and respect for procedures. * Hands\-on attitude and willingness to work in an on\-site technical environment. * Reliability, punctuality, ownership, and attention to detail. * Clear communication and timely escalation when issues or risks appear. * Teamwork across IT operations, facility, security, asset management, vendors, and customer\-side teams. * A learning mindset. Candidates do not need to know everything from day one, but they must be able to follow SOPs carefully and grow with the project. title: IT Technical Architect

Ornua has revenues of €3\.4 billion and is supported by a global team of approximately 2,800 employees. The Group operates as a commercial organisation across 10 business units, including 12 production facilities located in Europe, North America, the Middle East and Africa. The commercial organisation is responsible for the marketing and sales of Ornua’s consumer brands including Ireland’s most successful food export: Kerrygold. It also manages the procurement of Irish and non\-Irish dairy products, the sale of dairy ingredients to food manufacturing and foodservice customers globally, and the implementation of de\-risking and trading strategies to manage market volatility. These activities are supported by production facilities and in\-market teams in Europe, North America, the Middle East and Africa. **Job Summary** As a Technical Architect, you will be working in the Global IT Infrastructure Services team. developing, designing and implementing modern infrastructure and security solutions and working with other departments to integrate SaaS solutions into existing technologies. The successful candidate will work closely within Infrastructure, Security, Service Delivery and Application teams to ensure all solutions are aligned with Ornua’s strategy and architecture ongoing management of solutions. * Deliver 3rd\-tier support and advanced troubleshooting for Azure\-based and on\-premises systems, optimising performance and reliability. **Key Skills \& Requirements** **:** * 5 years’ experience in a Technical Architect role. * Excellent knowledge in architecting, configuring, and deploying solutions across Microsoft Azure IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS services. * Detailed knowledge of on\-premises infrastructure components (Server, Storage, Network, Network Security, and Active Directory). * Demonstrated knowledge of identity, authentication, and enterprise application management with Entra ID. * Experience in drafting technical documentation and explaining technical problems and solutions for both technical and non\-technical audiences. * Experience managing service providers and other project stakeholders. * Proven commitment to continuous technical development. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including the ability to clearly communicate technical issues to a non\-technical audience. * Experience architecting and implementing solutions within a global enterprise environment * Strong attention to detail. * Experienced in the design and delivery of enterprise level Highly Available solutions. * Understanding of project delivery and service delivery methodology. * Experience working on information security related projects. * Microsoft Azure certification and TOGAF would be an advantage. **Key competencies** * INTEGRITY: Supports and encourages constructive outcomes for others by demonstrating OWNERSHIP: Takes responsibility for and pride in delivering quality and results; is tenacious. title: Infection Control Nurse (JD-0050)
description: **Overview:** The Infection Control Nurse will play a key role in safeguarding patient and staff safety in a multi\-specialty hospital setting. The role focuses on infection prevention initiatives, outbreak management, and maintaining the highest standards of hospital epidemiology. **Key Responsibilities \& Tasks** * Develop, implement, and monitor infection control policies to ensure compliance with best practices, statutory regulations, and hospital standards. * Conduct regular clinical audits to evaluate adherence to infection prevention protocols and recommend corrective actions where necessary. * Establish and maintain comprehensive surveillance systems to monitor infection rates, trends, and outcomes across all hospital departments. * Lead and coordinate the investigation and management of hospital\-acquired infection outbreaks, ensuring timely containment and accurate reporting. * Educate clinical and support staff on infection control measures through structured training sessions, workshops, and ongoing communication initiatives. * Collaborate with hospital management, quality, and clinical teams to develop and implement strategies for enhancing patient safety and infection prevention. * Analyse surveillance data, prepare reports, and present findings to relevant stakeholders to support evidence\-based decision\-making. * Participate in accreditation and compliance activities, contributing to adherence with local, national, and international infection control standards. * Act as a subject matter expert, providing guidance on infection prevention practices during new clinical program launches, risk assessments, and policy updates. * Mentor and support junior staff, fostering professional growth, developing infection control competencies, and promoting continuous performance improvement. **Qualifications \& Experience** * GNM / B.Sc. Nursing with diploma/ certificate course in infection control from a recognized institution and Delhi Nursing Council Registration. * Minimum 10 years of professional nursing experience out of which atleast 5 years as ICN in a 200 or more bedded hospital. * Strong expertise in Infection Control, including developing, implementing, and monitoring protocols to reduce healthcare\-associated infections. * Proven knowledge in Hospital Epidemiology, with the ability to understand disease transmission patterns and implement effective preventive strategies. * Proficient in surveillance systems to monitor, analyze, and report infection trends, enabling timely interventions and continuous improvement. * Experienced in clinical auditing to evaluate compliance with infection control policies and provide actionable recommendations to healthcare teams. * Committed to patient safety initiatives, including staff training, procedural reviews, and ongoing education on infection prevention practices. * Skilled in outbreak management, coordinating rapid response, containment measures, and interdepartmental communication during infection incidents. * Excellent communication skills for educating staff, reporting findings, and providing updates to patients, families, and clinical teams. * Detail\-oriented with strong analytical and data\-driven decision\-making skills to support evidence\-based infection control standards. * Ability to mentor and guide teams, fostering a culture of safety, compliance, and adherence to current infection control guidelines. **Remuneration:** As per industry standards **Why Join Us:** At Sant Nirankari Health City, we offer a collaborative and supportive work environment where your contributions are valued and recognized. Join us in our mission to deliver exceptional healthcare services while upholding the highest standards of quality and patient care. our mission to deliver exceptional healthcare services while upholding the highest standards of quality and patient care. Apply Now ← Back to Careers Page

title: Information Technology Architect
description: **Job Description** **Job Title:** IT Technical Architect **Department:** IT **Reports To:** Global IT Infrastructure Director \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_ Ornua is a leading dairy co\-operative which sells premium dairy products globally on behalf of its Member Co\-operatives, Ireland’s dairy processors and, in turn, Irish dairy farmers. Ornua has revenues of €3\.4 billion and is supported by a global team of approximately 2,800 employees. The Group operates as a commercial organisation across 10 business units, including 12 production facilities located in Europe, North America, the Middle East and Africa. The commercial organisation is responsible for the marketing and sales of Ornua’s consumer brands including Ireland’s most successful food export: Kerrygold. Consumer markets are served by production facilities in Ireland, Germany and the UK and by in\-market Sales \& Marketing Teams in Asia, Germany, Ireland, MEA, Poland, Spain, rest of Europe and North and Latin America. It also manages the procurement of Irish and non\-Irish dairy products, the sale of dairy ingredients to food manufacturing and foodservice customers globally, and the implementation of de\-risking and trading strategies to manage market volatility. These activities are supported by production facilities and in\-market teams in Europe, North America, the Middle East and Africa. **Job Summary** As a Technical Architect, you will be working in the Global IT Infrastructure Services team. developing, designing and implementing modern infrastructure and security solutions and working with other departments to integrate SaaS solutions into existing technologies. The successful candidate will work closely within Infrastructure, Security, Service Delivery and Application teams to ensure all solutions are aligned with Ornua’s strategy and architecture principles. **Key Responsibilities** * Architect and deliver end\-to\-end solutions within the Microsoft Azure ecosystem, ensuring alignment with enterprise standards and security best practices. * Collaborate with business stakeholders to deeply understand functional and technical requirements and translate them into scalable, maintainable architectures. * Engage with implementation teams to ensure successful delivery of solutions, providing technical oversight and acting as an escalation point for complex issues or clarifications. * Create and maintain comprehensive technical documentation, including architecture diagrams, deployment procedures, and operational runbooks. * Provide knowledge transfer and upskilling to internal IT and support teams to ensure effective ongoing management of solutions. * Deliver 3rd\-tier support and advanced troubleshooting for Azure\-based and on\-premises systems, optimising performance and reliability. **Key Skills \& Requirements:** * 5 years’ experience in a Technical Architect role. * Excellent knowledge in architecting, configuring, and deploying solutions across Microsoft Azure IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS services. * Detailed knowledge of on\-premises infrastructure components (Server, Storage, Network, Network Security, and Active Directory). * Demonstrated knowledge of identity, authentication, and enterprise application management with Entra ID. * Experience in drafting technical documentation and explaining technical problems and solutions for both technical and non\-technical audiences. * Experience managing service providers and other project stakeholders. * Proven commitment to continuous technical development. * Strong communication and interpersonal skills, including the ability to clearly communicate technical issues to a non\-technical audience. * Experience architecting and implementing solutions within a global enterprise environment * Strong attention to detail. * Experienced in the design and delivery of enterprise level Highly Available solutions. * Understanding of project delivery and service delivery methodology. * Experience working on information security related projects. * Microsoft Azure certification and TOGAF would be an advantage. **Key competencies** * **date: 16 June 2026INTEGRITY:** Supports and encourages constructive outcomes for others by demonstrating integrity. * **Working Together:** Encourages co\-operation in own team and ensures cross\-functional working. * **Forward\-looking:** Responds positively to change and looks ahead to anticipate task. * **OWNERSHIP:** Takes responsibility for and pride in delivering quality and results; is tenacious. Closing date: 16 June 2026

title: Inside Sales Executive - Airbnb
description: **About MarketStar:** In everything we do, we believe in creating growth, for our clients, our employees, and our community. For the past 35\+ years, we have been generating revenue for the most innovative tech companies across the globe through our outsourced B2B demand, sales, customer success, and revenue operations solutions. We are passionate about cultivating career advancements for our people, and support them through mentorship, leadership, and career\-development programs. We provide service and support to our communities through the MarketStar Foundation. Our exceptional team is the cornerstone of MarketStar's accomplishments. We are proud of our award\-winning workplace culture and to be named a top employer in our industry. These achievements are a testament to our six core values, embraced by our 3,000\+ employees worldwide. From our headquarters in Utah, USA, to our global offices in India, Ireland, Bulgaria, Mexico, the Philippines, and Australia, we all work together to drive innovation and success. **About The Inside Sales Executive:** MarketStar is looking for an Inside Sales Executive for an exciting new project. In this role you will be identifying and recruiting hosts for our client's growing business. The perfect candidate will possess a deep knowledge of the client's platform and exceptional networking abilities. Your responsibilities will include researching prospects, initiating contact, and closing new business via phone and video conference. This is B2B sales with SME and Sole Trader and short sales cycles of up to 1 week. **About Our Client:** Our client is one of the largest players in the hospitality space, acting as a broker for 250 million happy customer transactions every year and turning over more than $10Billion in revenue in 2024\. This a household brand in the top 1,000 organisations in terms of revenue, listed as a Forbes Global 2000 company and featured in the TIME World’s Best Companies of 2024\. The potential in this project for a successful candidate is truly substantial! **Location:** Leopardstown, Dublin 18 \- Onsite (Mon \- Fri) **What will you do?** * Research and identify potential new customers * Reach out to prospects via email, phone, or social media * Conduct initial conversations to gauge interest and suitability * Manage ongoing communications until a prospect becomes a customer * Manage a book of prospects * Maintain accurate information within CRM systems and gdocs **What Will You Need to Succeed?** * 1 year of experience in recruitment, retail sales, cold calling, insurance sales, telecoms or utilities sales, general transactional B2C or B2C sales or B2B sales development * Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities. * Strong organizational and time\-management skills. * Proactive and self\-motivated with a results\-driven approach. * Flexibility to adapt to changing priorities and business needs. **What We Offer:** In our commitment to our “We Care” value, we believe in providing employees with valuable mental and physical well\-being benefits including: * Comprehensive VHI Health cover from day one * Structured training \& career development opportunities * Employer Matched Pension Scheme \& Education reimbursement (after 6 months) * Paid birthday leave * Personal Hardship Loan Program * Mental health and 24/7 employee assistance program * Bike To Work Scheme / Taxsaver Leap Card Scheme * Top\-performer and tenure awards *The pay for this position is basic salary per annum of €38,000 with an additional variable bonus component of €6,000 per annum. There are several factors to consider including but not limited to, the role’s responsibilities, experience, location, education/training, internal equity, and key skills. Your recruiter will provide more detailed information during the interview process.* MarketStar is firmly committed to Equal Employment Opportunity (EEO) and to compliance with all federal, state, and local laws that prohibit employment discrimination. Reasonable accommodations are available on request for candidates taking part in all aspects of the selection process. If you are an individual who identifies as someone with a disability and require reasonable accommodation(s) to complete any part of the job application process, please contact us at *people.success@marketstar.com* for assistance.

title: Installation & Qualification Technician
description: **WG** NSTAR is a dynamic and growing company with a global footprint. Primarily focused on the semiconductor industry, we provide a platform for you to expand your career with knowledge gained on the job training, and tailored development. We know we need talented people like you that hold similar values, which is why we do not put limits on learning, development, industry, and personal growth. Start your path to a **WG** NSTAR career today! **The Purpose of the Role** As an Installation \& Qualification Technician, you will be supporting the Customer in key areas throughout the manufacturing process. Your role will be centered around adhering to facility standard operating procedures with a focus on safety and quality. **Person Specification** * 3\-5 years' experience in a manufacturing or similar environment * Semiconductor experience is an advantage * Previous experience in Equipment Installation role is a distinct advantage * Participation in project improvement programmes * Experience in mechanical, electrical or maintenance positions * Good working knowledge of MS package * A strong team player with ability to work well within a diverse team * Have excellent organisational, time keeping, and communication skills * Ability to adapt to different tasks and to prioritise activities * You are looking for a new challenge and to develop your career **Work Schedule** 39\-hour regular working week (Monday to Friday) **Principal Duties And Responsibilities** * Initial equipment auditing and inspection * Monitor and review equipment un\-packaging and move in activities * Connection and testing of equipment sub\-assemblies * Preparation of handover documentation for Supplier Qualification process * Maintaining cleanroom and company protocols * Ensuring safety and quality standards are maintained * Performing quality checks in line with process specifications * Design package reviews and field verification * Liaise with trades contractors and support groups to ensure achievement of installation schedule milestones * Preparation of manufacturing equipment for safety level sign off * Management of facilities turn on requests and connection of critical services * Ownership of all documentation pertaining to connection, testing, and energizing of inert, toxic, non\-hazardous, and hazardous services * Accurate reporting of all items impacting safety, schedule, and budget of complex equipment installation activities **Role Requirements** * Level 8 qualification in engineering * Work in a controlled cleanroom environment, which will involve the wearing of a gown and relevant personal protective equipment * 100% compliance with all safety and protocol specs and regulations * Maintaining cleanroom and company protocols * Performing quality checks in line with process specifications * Using MS Office and in\-house computer system as required **Physical Requirements** * Have strong physical stamina to have the ability to stand, stoop, walk and reach for the duration of an 8\-hour shift (breaks excluded) **Benefits Of Working With WGNSTAR** * Discounted rates of on\-site facilities * Free on\-site car parking * Reward \& Recognition Programme * Employee Assistance Programme * Learning and development * Opportunities for career progression This job description is intended to convey information essential to understanding the scope of the job and the general nature and level of work performed by job holders within this job. This job description is not intended to be an exhaustive list of qualifications, skills, efforts, duties, responsibilities or working conditions associated with the position. WGNSTAR does not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies. Thank you. **Equal Opportunities Employer** **WG** NSTAR is an Equal Opportunity Employer in accordance with The Employment Equality Acts. **WG** NSTAR places strong emphasis on equal employment, encourage diversity in the workplace, and apply an open\-door policy for inclusion of all employees. Our diversity program encourages people from any gender, background, ethnicity, culture, education, and experience to join the company and more importantly build a career through employee development.

title: Investment Associate
description: Cairn is recruiting for an Investment Associate to join our team. **Responsibilities:** * Support underwriting of acquisitions and existing development opportunities including financial modelling, investment memos, deal structuring and due diligence. * Providing accurate financial modelling and scenario planning to enable effective decision making across the portfolio. * Work cross functionally in compiling, preparing and finalising development appraisals and modelling to support key decision making. * Support the Investment Team in gateway review stages, ensuring that financial, commercial, and design teams are aligned before presentation to senior leadership team members. * Support the long‑term land pipeline planning through site‑by‑site optimisation \& exit strategy * Assist in preparing weekly functional presentations for senior leadership team. * Conduct market research including researching market trends, competitor positioning, and policy changes influencing acquisition strategy and broader portfolio positioning. **Requirements:** * Degree or relevant experience in real estate, finance, economics etc. * Strong financial modelling capability. * Keen interest in development. * 3\-5 years’ experience. * Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills. * Strong presentation skills. * Strong problem solving and attention to detail\-orientation. * Highly commercial, proactive and comfortable working in an entrepreneurial environment. **Working with Cairn Homes** At Cairn, we believe it is essential that our employees feel valued and appreciated. We therefore offer above industry average remuneration packages with a comprehensive benefits portfolio that’s ready to support you for whatever life brings. We are delighted to offer a suite of benefits when you join Cairn which include * Competitive salary * Performance related bonus * Pension scheme with matching contributions * Full health insurance with Irish Life health * Annual salary review * Continuous Professional development (CPD) programme \& funding * Paid maternity \& paternity leave * Free Cairn annual leave days * Life assurance * Income protection * Employee Assistance Programme with VHI * Tax saver \- travel and bike to work * Culture Committee * Talent Development Programmes * Approved Profit Sharing Scheme (APSS) The Cairn purpose is to build sustainable communities where people can thrive. At Cairn, it’s not what we build, it’s why we build. It’s about putting down a marker that will stand for generations to come. Creating new communities of connection and belonging for an Ireland where people can thrive. Reshaping, redefining, reinvigorating our place in the world. Building for people, progress, and potential. Because when Cairn build, it’s Built For Good.

title: Junior Accounts Payable Specialist - Dublin - 145169
description: **Company Description** **Jones Engineering** are a leading engineering services company with a proven track record of high quality, customer focused project delivery for over 130 years. Our core divisions include Mechanical Engineering, Electrical Engineering, Sprinkler and Fire Protection. Some of the other services we provide include Specialist Lift, Off Site Fabrication, Technical Support Services, Maintenance, Asset Management, BIM (Building Information Modelling) and Geo\-Surveying. We offer competitive salaries, financial support for further training, performance incentives and the ability to progress your career with a world class engineering contractor. **Job Description** **Jones Engineering** are looking to hire an AP Specialist to join our team. This is a full\-time role based onsite at our headquarters in Pembroke Road, Dublin 4\. The ideal candidate will have a good understanding of the Accounts Payable process within a high\-volume, multinational environment. They will join the AP finance team and work within a team and report to the Finance Manager **In this role, You will;** * Process invoices/credit notes and expenses using SAP system * Dealing with vendor queries in a timely manner * Completion of vendor reconciliations * Process monthly employee expenses * Preparation of payment proposals for weekly payment run * Process changes to vendor account set up * Work with purchasing and commercial team to resolve invoice issues * Ensure general ledger postings are coded correctly * Ad\-hoc projects as required **For this role you will need;** * Candidates must have a minimum 2 years’ experience in Accounts Payable * Third level qualification or equivalent * Experience in a multinational environment with high invoice volumes * Excellent communication skills both verbal and written * Good team player with a strong willingness to participate and help others * Strong Excel skills and overall computer skills required **Benefits** We offer a competitive salary package commensurate with experience and qualifications. In addition, you will have the opportunity to work in a dynamic and supportive environment, collaborating with industry leaders and contributing to a sustainable future. Our company values work\-life balance and encourages professional growth through training and development programs. If you are ready to make a difference and be part of a growing industry, please submit your CV, along with a cover letter highlighting your relevant experience and why you are interested in this role. Recognising the contributions and respecting our people is core to our culture and values. We are an equal opportunities employer, and we encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply for roles.

title: Junior Brand and Product Designer
description: At **Manna** , we're changing how the world receives things — by taking delivery to the skies. Our mission is simple but ambitious: **to make high\-speed suburban delivery affordable, green, and safe** . We design and build our own aviation\-grade drones that deliver food, coffee, groceries, and more directly from local stores and vendors to people's doors in just a few minutes. ﻿We're a team that **Drive with Impact** , are **Safety Focused** , and knows that the best ideas come from **Team Players** who show up as their **Authentic Selves** . We move fast, we are passionate about what we do, and we're always pushing a growth mindset to **be better** in everything we do. If you want to build world\-changing technology with real\-world impact (and have a bit of fun while doing it), you'll love it here. ﻿ **Welcome to Manna — where we deliver the future.** ﻿ **Role Purpose** We are hiring a Junior Brand and Product Designer. This is not a "make things look nice" role. You'll work across brand, product, and growth—helping us acquire customers, improve the experience, and make Manna feel unmistakably different from anything else in delivery. You'll collaborate closely with design, product, marketing, and operations. You'll ship real work, fast. **Responsibilities** You will contribute to a wide range of design deliverables across both brand and product design, including but not limited to print materials, social media assets, marketing campaigns, email communications, digital products, and the processes that support them. Key responsibilities include: * Designing and producing high\-quality creative assets across print and digital channels * Creating medium\- and long\-form documents, presentations, and marketing materials * Developing scalable, templated design solutions for use by the design team and the wider business * Designing event stands, exhibition booths, and associated assets to support field marketing activities, including promotional merchandise, gifts, and social content * Ensuring the Manna brand is represented consistently and to the highest standard across all touchpoints * Researching, generating, and developing creative concepts and visual directions for projects * Continuously developing your skills through experience, training, and industry best practice * Staying informed about emerging design trends, technologies, and creative production techniques * Learning new tools, techniques, and processes as project requirements evolve * Seeking guidance and feedback when needed to ensure work remains aligned with project objectives * Communicating proactively and clearly with stakeholders, colleagues, and project teams * Contributing to product design initiatives across Manna's customer\-facing applications and internal operational tools * Translating user needs, business requirements, and research insights into wireframes, user flows, prototypes, and polished user interfaces * Supporting the development and maintenance of Manna's design system, ensuring consistency across components, patterns, and user experiences * Designing solutions that balance user needs, business objectives, and technical considerations **Activities** **Brand \& Marketing Design** * Creating high\-quality layouts and typography across a diverse range of outputs * Working creatively within established Manna brand guidelines while identifying opportunities to evolve and strengthen the brand * Managing projects independently and contributing effectively as part of the wider design team * Participating in design critiques, reviews, and collaborative workshops * Working closely with colleagues across departments to deliver impactful design solutions * Collaborating with external agencies, suppliers, and production partners when required * Managing priorities effectively and maintaining ownership of your workload **Product Design** * Designing intuitive user experiences through user flows, wireframing, prototyping, and high\-fidelity interface design * Creating experiences for both mobile and web platforms * Contributing to and working within Manna's design system and component libraries * Collaborating closely with Product Managers and Engineers to deliver new features and continuously improve existing experiences * Designing with accessibility, usability, and scalability at the forefront * Using customer insights, data, and feedback to inform design decisions and iterations **Requirements** We're looking for someone who: * Has a genuine passion for design and takes pride in delivering high\-quality work * Continuously looks for opportunities to improve processes, experiences, and creative output * Is self\-motivated, proactive, and comfortable taking initiative * Is dependable and consistently delivers on commitments and deadlines * Has strong proficiency in core design tools and can confidently select the most appropriate tool for each task * Thrives in a fast\-paced environment and is comfortable managing multiple projects simultaneously * Has excellent attention to detail and maintains high standards across all aspects of design * Is comfortable working across both brand and product design disciplines * Has experience using Figma, including components, auto\-layout, design systems, and prototyping * Demonstrates a strong understanding of interface design principles, including hierarchy, spacing, interaction states, and edge cases * Is curious about how design impacts business and product performance, including customer activation, retention, engagement, and order frequency * Communicates effectively and collaborates positively with stakeholders at all levels **What We Ask Of Our Designers** **Ask Questions** No question is ever misplaced. We encourage curiosity, open discussion, and the sharing of ideas. Bring your thoughts, challenges, and suggestions to the team early and often. **Consider the Bigger Picture** Think about your work beyond the immediate task. Consider how each project strengthens the brand, improves the customer experience, and contributes to broader business goals. B **e Opinionated, but Open\-Minded** Bring a point of view and support your ideas with clear rationale. At the same time, remain open to feedback, new perspectives, and changing direction when needed. **Collaborate Generously** Design is a team sport. Share your work early, seek feedback regularly, and contribute constructively to critiques and discussions. Work closely with stakeholders and colleagues to achieve the best possible outcomes. **Embrace Continuous Improvement** Seek opportunities to learn, develop your skills, and refine your approach. Stay curious about emerging trends, technologies, and ways of working that can enhance both your craft and the team's impact.

title: Junior Consultant - Training Provided
description: Are you an ambitious professional looking to make a significant impact in a new and dynamic field? If you have a passion for people and thrive in fast\-paced environments, we have an exciting opportunity for you! * **Unlimited Earning Potential: Fast\-Paced \& Rewarding Environment** * **Clear and Transparent Career Development – Progress to Leadership** * **Personalised Training – Use your Sales skills in a new exciting way** **What can you expect day to day?** As an Associate Recruitment Consultant, you'll have the freedom to do things differently, supported by colleagues with a wealth of experience, empowering you to learn and grow. No day is the same here at Access, and you will gain experience across a varied range of skills including: * Building relationships with clients and candidates * Managing vacancies from start to finish * Screening, interviewing and shortlisting candidates in the healthcare industry Here at Access, you really can influence your own earning potential, by getting out what you put in. Your energy and dedication will be rewarded in a culture built on celebrating success. **What we are looking for** : * 1 – 2 years of professional experience (any industry can be considered) – No previous recruitment experience is needed. * Passion to succeed and be part of a fast\-paced inclusive environment * Relationship\-focused with the confidence to build strong networks * Collaborative in spirit with a growth mindset to develop a long\-lasting career **Benefits \& Perks** * Basic Salary \+ Uncapped Commission (Average OTE: Year 1 €45k, Year 2 €75k) * Internal Learning and development, helping support and train alongside the support of your manager * Clear path for progression: until 12 promotions available all the way to Equity Partner \+ Leadership Academy programme for managers in the making (all leaders grow inside) * Paid sabbatical * Wellness programme (e.g. time management, skills development, health talks, advise and activities, stress management) * Industry\-leading training programme with 200\+ training modules – Become a Certified Recruitment Consultant * Fantastic team culture, very collaborative and team socials If you are curious enough to know more, all you need to do is send us a current CV! francisca.simoes@accesshealthcare.ie ***Please note that only EU Citizens \& Stamp 4 applications will be considered for this role.***

title: Junior Data Analyst
description: Data Analyst 📍 Dublin \| Hybrid Working 💼 12 month contract \- Daily Rate About the Role An exciting opportunity has arisen for a Data Analyst to join a growing Data Analytics team within a leading global financial services organisation. This role offers the chance to work at the heart of data quality, analytics, and continuous improvement initiatives, supporting the integrity and management of critical security reference data. You'll combine day\-to\-day data analysis and quality assurance activities with project\-based work focused on improving processes, automation, and data\-driven decision making. This position would suit someone with 1–2 years' experience in data analytics, data management, or financial services, as well as recent graduates with strong analytical skills and relevant internships or project experience. Key Responsibilities * Perform data analysis to support the quality, accuracy, and integrity of business\-critical reference data. * Investigate data issues, identify root causes, and provide actionable insights and recommendations. * Monitor data quality and identify anomalies, trends, and areas for improvement. * Collaborate with business and technology teams to resolve data\-related challenges. * Support data management and governance initiatives across the organisation. * Develop and maintain reports, dashboards, and analytical outputs to support business decision\-making. * Contribute to process improvement initiatives aimed at increasing efficiency and data quality. * Support the adoption of automation, anomaly detection, and AI\-driven solutions to enhance data management capabilities. * Participate in a variety of analytical, operational, and project\-based initiatives. Skills \& Experience Essential * Bachelor's Degree in Business, Finance, Data Analytics, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related discipline. * 1–2 years' experience in a Data Analyst, Data Management, or similar analytical role. * Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills with the ability to investigate complex data issues. * Experience working with data management, data quality, or reference data. * Working knowledge of: * SQL * Python or R * Power BI or similar visualisation tools * Strong communication skills with the ability to explain technical concepts to non\-technical stakeholders. * Excellent organisational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities. Desirable * Experience within financial services or a highly data\-driven environment. * Exposure to market data or reference data platforms. * Familiarity with Bloomberg, Reuters, or similar data vendor products. * Experience with automation, anomaly detection, machine learning, or AI\-driven analytics solutions. * Understanding of data governance and data quality frameworks. What We're Looking For * A naturally curious and proactive mindset. * Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality. * Ability to work independently while also collaborating effectively within a team environment. * Interest in innovation, continuous improvement, and emerging technologies. * Strong stakeholder management and relationship\-building skills. * Passion for using data to solve business problems and drive better outcomes. title: Learning Coordinator
description: **Building a sustainable tomorrow** ----------------------------------- Reporting to the Learning \& Talent Manager, as Learning Coordinator, you’ll play a key part in supporting the delivery of our programmes, from apprenticeships to undergraduate \& graduate programmes, it is an exciting role! You’ll be the go\-to person for administrative, coordination, and logistical support, helping to make sure everything is well\-organised, our data is up to date, and communication flows seamlessly between teams, candidates, and external partners. In short, you’ll be a core part of the team that helps shape the future talent of BAM. This role is based in our Head Office in Kildare, with some travel in Ireland during the academic year where required. We also offer Hybrid and flexible working. **What’s in it for you?** ------------------------- Opportunities! Opportunities to work with a great team on some of Irelands largest, dynamic and most exciting construction, civil and FM projects. Opportunity to develop your skills to the highest industry standards at one of the country's largest construction firms. We are committed to our employees and offer strong competitive salaries along with great benefits. * 8% pension contribution. * Private Health Insurance. * Dental Plan. * 26 days annual leave, incremental with years service. * 2 Wellbeing days annually. * Paid maternity \& paternity leave. * Learning \& Development opportunities \- internal progression is encouraged and fostered. * Company vehicles (role dependent). * .....and many more great perks. **Making Possible** ------------------- As part of the Learning team, you'll play a vital role in supporting the smooth delivery of programmes across BAM UK\&I. Your responsibilities will include: **Programme Coordination:** * Provide high\-quality administrative support for early careers programmes including apprenticeships, graduates, and internships. Maintain accurate records, track progress, and ensure compliance documentation is up to date. **Recruitment \& Onboarding Support:** * Assist with recruitment activities, onboarding processes, and coordination of events such as inductions, training sessions, and workshops. **Stakeholder Communication:** * Act as the main point of contact for early careers queries, supporting internal teams, candidates, training providers, and partners. Help deliver the communication and engagement strategy across programmes. **Event \& Campaign Support:** * Coordinate logistics for events like interviews, assessment centres, and internal campaigns. Manage venue bookings, travel arrangements, and resources. **Data, Compliance \& Reporting:** * Support data entry, compliance tracking, audit preparation, and budget administration. Assist in reporting on KPIs and programme outcomes. **Continuous Improvement:** * Gather feedback, support communications through newsletters and digital content, and contribute ideas to enhance the early careers experience. Essential Experience. **What do you bring to the role?** ---------------------------------- * 1 year \+ Strong administrative or coordination experience/exposure, ideally within HR, L\&D, Recruitment, or Early Careers. * Excellent organisational skills, with the ability to manage multiple tasks and deadlines. * Strong interpersonal and communication skills, with a customer\-service mindset. * Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, Outlook, Word) and data management systems. * Experience working in a team environment and supporting a range of stakeholders. * Impeccable attention to detail. **About BAM** ------------- Building a sustainable tomorrow. That’s our mission and our promise at BAM. It’s how we engineer vital infrastructure and construct high\-quality buildings as one of the largest construction companies in Europe. We strive to create an environment where everybody feels welcome and valued. We’re on an exciting journey to employ the best talent to join us regardless of social background, race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability or other characteristics. **The application process** --------------------------- BAM is committed to ensuring a fully inclusive recruitment and onboarding process, so if at any time you feel we need to do something to make it more accessible to you, do not hesitate to let us know. Click the link to apply and Join us in making possible \#ie1

Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions.
